functions.name: DNSLookup.Address
functions.description: Queries the address.

parameters.name: LookupRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the DNS Lookup.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the address. From 0 to DNSLookup.AddressCount -1. Index of -1 gives list with all values.

examples.label: Query first address Apple.com:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
d =
MBS
("
DNSLookup.LookupHostByName
"; "www.apple.com"; 6);
r =
MBS
("DNSLookup.Address"; d; 0);
e =
MBS
("
DNSLookup.Release
"; d)
]; r )
Example result:
2a02:26f0:ab00:38d::1aca
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
  d = MBS("DNSLookup.LookupHostByName"; "www.apple.com"; 6);
  r = MBS("DNSLookup.Address"; d; 0);
  e = MBS("DNSLookup.Release"; d)
]; r )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DNSLookup.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of DNSLookup objects. Returns IDs for all DNSLookup objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call DNSLookup.Release in your scripts.

examples.label: List all object IDs:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DNSLookup.List" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DNSLookup.LookupHostByAddress
functions.description: Queries the domain name for an IP. Lookup is synchronous.

parameters.name: IP
parameters.description: The IP address to query.

examples.label: Lookup an IP:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$q
; Value:
MBS
( "DNSLookup.LookupHostByAddress";
DNS Lookup::IP
to lookup )]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0]
Set Field [
DNS Lookup::Name
;
MBS
( "
DNSLookup.Name
";
$q
)]
Set Field [
DNS Lookup::Addresses
;
MBS
( "
DNSLookup.Address
";
$q
; -1 )]
Set Field [
DNS Lookup::Aliases
;
MBS
( "
DNSLookup.Alias
";
$q
; -1 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DNSLookup.Release
";
$q
)]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DNSLookup.LookupHostByAddress"; DNS Lookup::IP to lookup )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$q</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError") = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "DNSLookup.Name"; $q )</Calculation>
    <Field table="DNS Lookup" id="99999" name="Name"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "DNSLookup.Address"; $q; -1 )</Calculation>
    <Field table="DNS Lookup" id="99999" name="Addresses"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "DNSLookup.Alias"; $q; -1 )</Calculation>
    <Field table="DNS Lookup" id="99999" name="Aliases"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DNSLookup.Release"; $q)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DNSLookup.LookupHostByName
functions.description: Queries the IP for domain. Lookup is synchronous. By default queries the primary IP type the machine is configured for.

parameters.name: DomainName
parameters.description: The domain name to lookup.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: What IP type to find. Can be 4 for IPv4 or 6 for IPv6. Default is IPv4. This is currently ignored on Windows and you get back default IP type.

examples.label: Lookup domain name:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$q
; Value:
MBS
( "DNSLookup.LookupHostByName";
DNS Lookup::Name
to lookup; Get(ScriptParameter))]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0]
Set Field [
DNS Lookup::Name
;
MBS
( "
DNSLookup.Name
";
$q
)]
Set Field [
DNS Lookup::Addresses
;
MBS
( "
DNSLookup.Address
";
$q
; -1 )]
Set Field [
DNS Lookup::Aliases
;
MBS
( "
DNSLookup.Alias
";
$q
; -1 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DNSLookup.Release
";
$q
)]
End If

functions.name: EnvironmentVariables.SetValue
functions.description: Sets an environment variable. Only visible within FileMaker application and subprocesses launched after the change. For Python on macOS or Linux, you can use EnvironmentVariables.SetValue to set variables before you load python. But for Windows this won't work.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the environment variable.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value as text.

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ErrorLog
functions.description: Queries error log. Error log gets activated with ClearErrors , which also clears all entries. Please call ClearErrors function on beginning of a script and then on the end of the script, use HadErrors function to check if there was a problem. Use this function to query JSON with error details. Querying the error log using ErrorLog function turns it off until the next call to ClearErrors function. You can add own entries with AddToErrorLog function. Errors are tracked per thread, so on a server each PSoS session has its own error log. And it includes errors from subscripts. Our error log is in addition to trace functions and Plugin.SetErrorScriptTrigger function.

examples.label: Try the error log:
examples.text_code:
# test Errors in file Kontakte
# start error logging
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("ClearErrors") ]
#
# cause some errors
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("test"; 1; "Hello") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.Print
"; 1; "Hello") ]
#
# now query error log
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("ErrorLog") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error Log" ;
$r
]
Example result:
[
	{
"function"
:
"test"
,
"result"
:
"[MBS] Unknown function: test"
,
"parameter"
:	[
"test"
,
1
,
"Hello"
],
"fileName"
:
"Kontakte"
,
"scriptName"
:
"test Errors"
,
"currentTimeStamp"
:
"23.06.2022 09:28:34"
}, 
	{
"function"
:
"DynaPDF.Print"
,
"result"
:
"[MBS] DynaPDF.Print is only supported for Windows. Please use PDFKit.Print on Mac."
,
"parameter"
:	[
"DynaPDF.Print"
,
1
,
"Hello"
],
"fileName"
:
"Kontakte"
,
"scriptName"
:
"test Errors"
,
"currentTimeStamp"
:
"23.06.2022 09:28:34"
}
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> test Errors in file Kontakte</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> start error logging</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ClearErrors") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cause some errors</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("test"; 1; "Hello") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Print"; 1; "Hello") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: Events.Alarm.SetAbsoluteDate
functions.description: Sets an alarm that fires at a specific date. Set this property to a date to establish an absolute alarm trigger. Setting this clears any relative interval trigger.

parameters.name: EKAlarm
parameters.description: The reference to the alarm.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp to fire the alarm.

functions.name: Events.Alarm.SetEmailAddress
functions.description: Sets the email for this alarm. An email address that is the recipient of an email alarm, which is an alarm that triggers an email message. When you set the emailAddress, the type is set to Email,  and the soundName and url are cleared.

parameters.name: EKAlarm
parameters.description: The reference to the alarm.

parameters.name: Email
parameters.description: The new email address.

functions.name: Events.Alarm.SetProximity
functions.description: Sets the proximity for this alarm. Defines whether this alarm triggers via entering/exiting a geofence as defined by structuredLocation.

parameters.name: EKAlarm
parameters.description: The reference to the alarm.

parameters.name: Proximity
parameters.description: The proximity. Can be None, Leave or Enter.

functions.name: Events.Alarm.SetRelativeOffset
functions.description: Sets a relative offset from an event start date to fire an alarm. Set this property to an appropriate negative value to establish an alarm trigger relative to the start date/time of an event. Setting this clears any existing date trigger. Value is in seconds.

parameters.name: EKAlarm
parameters.description: The reference to the alarm.

parameters.name: TimeOffset
parameters.description: The relative time offset in seconds.

examples.label: Add an alaram with relative offset:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$alarm
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.NewAlarm
" ) ]
If [ not IsEmpty(
Test::AlarmSound
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Alarm.SetSound
";
$alarm
;
Test::AlarmSound
) ]
End If
If [ not IsEmpty(
Test::AlarmLocationTitle
) ]
Set Variable [
$l
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.StructuredLocation.NewLocationWithTitle
";
Test::AlarmLocationTitle
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.StructuredLocation.SetRadius
";
$l
;
Test::AlarmLocationRadius
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.StructuredLocation.SetGeoLocation
";
$l
;
Test::AlarmLocationGeoLat
;
Test::AlarmLocationGeoLong
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Alarm.SetStructuredLocation
";
$alarm
;
$l
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Alarm.SetProximity
";
$alarm
;
Test::AlarmProximity
) ]
End If
# e.g. AlarmRelativeOffsetSeconds contains -9 * 60
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Events.Alarm.SetRelativeOffset";
$alarm
; Evaluate(
Test::AlarmRelativeOffsetSeconds
) ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Item.AddAlarm
";
$item
;
$alarm
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.NewAlarm" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$alarm</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> not IsEmpty(Test::AlarmSound) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Alarm.SetSound"; $alarm; Test::AlarmSound ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> not IsEmpty(Test::AlarmLocationTitle) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.StructuredLocation.NewLocationWithTitle"; Test::AlarmLocationTitle ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.StructuredLocation.SetRadius"; $l; Test::AlarmLocationRadius ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.StructuredLocation.SetGeoLocation"; $l; Test::AlarmLocationGeoLat; Test::AlarmLocationGeoLong ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Alarm.SetStructuredLocation"; $alarm; $l) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Alarm.SetProximity"; $alarm; Test::AlarmProximity ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> e.g. AlarmRelativeOffsetSeconds contains -9 * 60</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Alarm.SetRelativeOffset"; $alarm; Evaluate( Test::AlarmRelativeOffsetSeconds ) ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Item.AddAlarm"; $item; $alarm ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.Alarm.SetSound

parameters.name: Sound

$alarm2
; Value:

( "
Events.NewAlarmWithRelativeOffset
"; -15*60 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Events.Alarm.SetSound";
$alarm2
; "Submarine" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Events.Item.AddAlarm
";
$c
;
$alarm2
)]

functions.name: Events.Alarm.SetURL
functions.description: Sets the URL to open when the alarm triggers. When you set the url property, the action property is set to Procedure, and the emailAddress and soundName properties are cleared. Note: Starting with OS X 10.9, it is not possible to create new procedure alarms or view URLs for existing procedure alarms. Trying to save or modify a procedure alarm will result in a save error. Editing other aspects of events or reminders that have existing procedure alarms is allowed as long as the alarm isn't modified.

parameters.name: EKAlarm
parameters.description: The reference to the alarm.

  </Step>

functions.name: Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType
functions.description: Returns the authorization status for the given entity type. You can check and see if your solution is allowed to use the calendar. Value is NotDetermined, Restricted, Denied or Authorized. You can use Events.RequestAccessToEntityType to request access.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Which entity type you like to check: Event or Reminder.

examples.label: Query authorization:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType"; "Reminder")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["We have a problem";
MBS
("
Text.RemovePrefix
";
$r
; "[
MBS
] ")]
Else If [
$r
= "NotDetermined"]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.RequestAccessToEntityType
"; "Reminder" )]
If [
$r
= "Wait"]
#dialog is showing.
Else If [
$r
= "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Success"; "We are authorized and ready to go."]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["We have a problem";
$r
]
End If
Else If [
$r
= "Restricted"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Success"; "We are authorized for a restricted access and ready to go."]
Else If [
$r
= "Denied"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Problem"; "We are denied for calendar access. Please go to system preferences and enable us."]
Else If [
$r
= "Authorized"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Success"; "We are authorized and ready to go."]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["We have a problem";
$r
]
End If

functions.name: Events.Calendar.AllowsContentModifications
functions.description: Represents whether you can this add, remove, or modify items in this calendar. Returns 1 if you can modify the calendar. This function checks for a license. Created 10th March 2016 , last changed 12nd March 2016 Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType - Events.Calendar.CalendarIdentifier 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: EKCalendar
parameters.description: The identifier or name of the calendar.

functions.name: Events.Calendar.CalendarIdentifier
functions.description: A unique identifier for the calendar. It is not sync-proof in that a fullsync will lose this identifier, so you should always have a back up plan for dealing with a calendar that is no longer fetchable by this property, e.g. by title, type, color, etc. This function should not needed unless you want to map a plugin given ID to a real calendar ID.

parameters.name: EKCalendar
parameters.description: The identifier or name of the calendar.

functions.name: Events.Calendar.GetSource
functions.description: Query the source ID for this calendar. The source representing the 'account' this calendar belongs to. This is only settable when initially creating a calendar and then effectively read-only after that. That is, you can create a calendar, but you cannot move it to another source. See Events.Sources to get a list of all sources.

parameters.name: EKCalendar
parameters.description: The identifier or name of the calendar.

functions.result: Returns source reference number or error.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.Calendar.GetTitle
functions.description: Gets the title of the calendar

parameters.name: EKCalendar
parameters.description: The identifier or name of the calendar.

examples.label: Query IDs and names for all:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of Calendar IDs
	list = MBS( "Events.Calendars"; "Event" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	id = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query name for each ID
	name = MBS( "Events.Calendar.GetTitle"; id); 
	names = names & id & " -> " & name & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.Calendar.GetType
functions.description: Queries type of calendar. This is actually based on what source the calendar is in, as well as whether it is a subscribed calendar. CalDAV subscribed calendars have type CalDAV with isSubscribed being true. Type can be: Local, CalDAV, Exchange, Birthday or Subscription.

parameters.name: EKCalendar
parameters.description: The identifier or name of the calendar.

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of Calendar IDs
	list = MBS( "Events.Calendars"; "Event" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	id = GetValue(list; index);

functions.name: Events.ClearNotification
functions.description: Clears the script trigger for changes.

functions.name: Events.Commit
functions.description: Commits pending changes to the database. If you use saveCalendar/saveEvent/removeCalendar/removeEvent, etc. and you pass 0 to their commit parameter, you are batching changes for a later commit. This method does that commit. This allows you to save the database only once for many additions or changes.  If you pass 1 to methods' commit parameter, then you don't need to call this method. This method will return OK as long as nothing went awry, even if nothing was actually committed. Else it returns an error.

functions.name: Events.CompletedReminders
functions.description: Fetch completed reminders in a set of calendars. You can use this method to search for reminders completed between a range of dates. You can pass nothing for start date to find all reminders completed before endDate. You can pass nothing for both start and end date to get all completed reminders in the specified calendars. You can pass nothing for calendars to fetch from all available calendars.

parameters.name: StartDate
parameters.description: The start date to search from. Can be empty.

parameters.name: EndDate
parameters.description: The end date to search before.

parameters.name: Calendars
parameters.description: The identifier for the calendar you want to search. Leave empty to search all.

functions.result: Returns list of reminder IDs or error.

functions.name: Events.DelegateSources
functions.description: Returns an unordered list of sources for all available delegates. By default, delegates are not included in an event store's sources. To work with delegates, you can create a new event store and pass in the sources, including sources returned from this method, that you're interested in.

parameters.name: AsJSON
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as JSON.

examples.label: Query delegate sources as JSON:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.Event.BirthdayContactIdentifier
functions.description: The contact identifier of the person for this birthday event. This property only applies to events in the built-in Birthdays calendar. It specifies the contact identifier (for use with the Contacts functions) of the person for whom the system created this event. For any other type of event, this property returns "".

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.result: Returns identifier for contact or error.

functions.name: Events.Event.EventIdentifier
functions.description: Queries event long identifier for a short event identifier. If the plugin assigned a short 5 digit event identifier to an event as part of a repeating event, you can query the long event ID here.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.GetAllDay
functions.description: Query whether this is an all day event.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.GetAvailability
functions.description: Queries availability. Can be NotSupported, Busy, Free, Tentative or Unavailable.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.GetEndDate
functions.description: Queries the end date for the event.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.GetStartDate
functions.description: Queries the start date for the event. This property represents the start date for this event. Floating events (such  as all-day events) are currently always returned in the default time zone.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.GetStructuredLocation
functions.description: Queries structured location. Allows you to set a structured location (a location with a potential geo-coordinate) on an event. The getter for Event’s location property just returns the structured location’s title. The setter for event’s location property is equivalent to creating a new structured location with just a title. Requires OS X 10.11 or newer.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.result: Returns structured location ID or error.

functions.name: Events.Event.IsDetached
functions.description: Represents whether this event is detached from a recurring series. If this event is an instance of a repeating event, and an attribute of this  event has been changed from the default value generated by the repeating event, isDetached will return 1. If the EKEvent is unchanged from its default state, or is not a repeating event, isDetached returns 0. This function checks for a license. Created 10th March 2016 , last changed 12nd March 2016 Events.Event.GetStructuredLocation - Events.Event.OccurrenceDate 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: EKCalendarItem
parameters.description: The reference to the calendar item: an event or reminder.

functions.name: Events.Event.OccurrenceDate
functions.description: The occurrence date of an event if it is part of a recurring series. This is only set if the event is part of a recurring series. It returns the date on which this event was originally scheduled to occur. For occurrences that are unmodified from the recurring series, this is the same as the start date. This value will remain the same even if the event has been detached and its start date has changed. Floating events (such as all-day events) are currently returned in the default time zone. This function checks for a license. Created 10th March 2016 , last changed 12nd March 2016 Events.Event.IsDetached - Events.Event.Refresh 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.Refresh
functions.description: Refreshes an event object to ensure it's still valid. When the database changes, your application is sent an notification note and the plugin may trigger the notification script. You should generally consider all event instances to be invalid as soon as you receive the notification. However, for events you truly care to keep around, you can call this method. It ensures the record is still valid by ensuring the event and start date are still valid. It also attempts to refresh all properties except those you might have modified. If this method returns 0, the record has been deleted or is otherwise invalid. You should not continue to use it. If it returns 1, all is still well, and the record is ready for continued use. You should only call this method on events that are more critical to keep around if possible, such as an event that is being actively edited, as this call is fairly heavyweight. Do not use it to refresh the entire selected range of events you might have had selected. It is mostly pointless anyway, as recurrence information may have changed.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.SetAllDay
functions.description: Sets the all day flag for an event.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Pass 1 for all day, else 0.

functions.name: Events.Event.SetAvailability
functions.description: Sets availability.

parameters.name: EKEvent

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description: The availability setting. Pass Busy, Free, Tentative or Unavailable.

functions.name: Events.Event.SetStructuredLocation

functions.name: Events.Event.Status
functions.description: Queries status of event. While the status offers four different values in the enumeration, in practice, the only actionable and reliable status is canceled. Any other status should be considered informational at best. You cannot set this property. If you wish to cancel an event, you should simply remove it using removeEvent. Value can be None, Confirmed, Tentative or Canceled. This function checks for a license. Created 10th March 2016 , last changed 12nd March 2016 Events.Event.SetStructuredLocation - Events.Event.compareStartDateWithEvent 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.compareStartDateWithEvent
functions.description: Comparison function. Allows you to sort events. Returns 1 if one second event is bigger, -1 if smaller or 0 if equal. This function checks for a license. Created 10th March 2016 , last changed 12nd March 2016 Events.Event.Status - Events.Event.organizer 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

parameters.name: OtherEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

functions.name: Events.Event.organizer
functions.description: The organizer of this event, or empty. Returns a participant ID or nothing. The plugin can't set the organizer as Apple doesn't provide API for this. This function checks for a license. Created 10th March 2016 , last changed 12nd March 2016 Events.Event.compareStartDateWithEvent - Events.EventStoreIdentifier 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: EKEvent

functions.name: Events.EventStoreIdentifier
functions.description: Returns a unique identifier string representing this calendar store. If this changes, your database got moved to a different Mac or the user started with a new calendar database. So existing identifiers for events are invalidated.

functions.name: Events.Events
functions.description: Queries events in a given time range. You can use this function to search for events between a range of dates. You can pass nothing for start date to find all events before endDate. You can pass nothing for both start and end date to get all events in the specified calendars. You can pass nothing for calendars to fetch from all available calendars. OS X Only: For performance reasons, this method will only return events within a four year timespan. If the date range between the startDate and endDate is greater than four years, then it will be shortened to the first four years. For events with occurrence dates, we return a 5 digit ID assigned by the plugin to distinguish between different occurrences.

parameters.name: StartDate
parameters.description: The start date to search from. Can be empty.

parameters.name: EndDate
parameters.description: The end date to search before.

parameters.name: Calendars
parameters.description: The identifier for the calendar you want to search. Leave empty to search all.

examples.label: Find events within next 24 hours:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Events"; get(CurrentHostTimestamp); get(CurrentHostTimestamp) + 24 * 60 * 60 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.IncompleteReminders

You can pass nothing for start date to find all reminders due before endDate. You can pass nothing for both start and end date to get all uncompleted reminders in the specified calendars. You can pass nothing for calendars to fetch from all available calendars.

parameters.name: StartDate

parameters.name: EndDate

parameters.name: Calendars

functions.name: Events.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes EventKit and opens event store. This is done internally automatically for reminders and events. You can here initialize it for only reminder or events. Users are able to grant or deny access to event and reminder data on a per-app basis. To request access to event and/or reminder data, instantiate an event store using this method. This call will not block the program while the user is being asked to grant or deny access. Until access has been granted for an entity type, this event store will not contain any calendars for that entity type, and any attempt to save entities of that entity type will fail. If access is later granted or declined, the event store will broadcast an notification. You can check the current access status for an entity type using Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType . The user will only be prompted the first time access is requested; any subsequent instantiations of event store will use the existing permissions.

parameters.name: Types
parameters.description: What entitiy types to use. Can be "Events", "Reminder" or both.

$alarm
; Value:

( "
Events.NewAlarm
" )]

";
$alarm
;

Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationTitle

$l
; Value:

( "
Events.StructuredLocation.NewLocationWithTitle
";
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationTitle
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.StructuredLocation.SetRadius
";
$l
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationRadius
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.StructuredLocation.SetGeoLocation
";
$l
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationGeoLat
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationGeoLong
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Alarm.SetStructuredLocation
";
$alarm
;
$l
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Alarm.SetProximity
";
$alarm
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmProximity
)]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Events.Item.AddAlarm";
$reminder
;
$alarm
)]

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>not IsEmpty(Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationTitle)</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.StructuredLocation.NewLocationWithTitle"; Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationTitle )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.StructuredLocation.SetRadius"; $l; Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationRadius )</Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Alarm.SetStructuredLocation"; $alarm; $l)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Alarm.SetProximity"; $alarm; Events Create Reminder::AlarmProximity )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Item.AddAlarm"; $reminder; $alarm )</Calculation>

functions.name: Events.Item.CalendarItemExternalIdentifier
functions.description: A server-provided identifier for this calendar item. This identifier, provided by the server, allows you to reference the same event or reminder across multiple devices. For calendars stored locally on the device, including the birthday calendar, it simply passes through to calendarItemIdentifier. This identifier is unique as of creation for every calendar item.  However, there are some cases where duplicate copies of a calendar item can exist in the same database, including: A calendar item was imported from an ICS file into multiple calendars An event was created in a calendar shared with the user and the user was also invited to the event The user is a delegate of a calendar that also has this event A subscribed calendar was added to multiple accounts In such cases, you should choose between calendar items based on other factors, such as the calendar or source. This identifier is the same for all occurrences of a recurring event. If you wish to differentiate between occurrences, you may want to use the start date. In addition, there are two caveats for Exchange-based calendars: This identifier will be different between EventKit on iOS versus OS X This identifier will be different between devices for Reminders You can later use Events.CalendarItemsWithExternalIdentifier function to get the local identifier back.

parameters.name: EKCalendarItem
parameters.description: The reference to the calendar item: an event or reminder.

functions.name: Events.Item.CalendarItemIdentifier
functions.description: Returns a unique identifier for a calendar item. Item identifiers are not sync-proof in that a full sync will lose this identifier, so you should always have a back up plan for dealing with a reminder that is no longer fetchable by this property, e.g. by title, etc.. You only need this function to match a reference number from our plugin to the UUID used by calendar identifier. See also Events.Item.CalendarItemExternalIdentifier .

Events.NewEvent

r =

g =

("
Events.Item.GetTimeZone

]; it & ¶ & r & ¶ & g )
Example result:
18A12594-0C4C-4BCE-8865-E36E2E044503
OK
Europe/London
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
it = MBS("Events.NewEvent");
r = MBS("Events.Item.SetTimeZone"; it; "Europe/London");
g = MBS("Events.Item.GetTimeZone"; it)

]; it & ¶ & r & ¶ & g )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.NewAlarm

examples.label: Add alarm:

$alarm
; Value:

( "Events.NewAlarm" )]
If [not IsEmpty(
Events Create Reminder::AlarmSound
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

( "
Events.Alarm.SetSound
";
$alarm
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmSound
)]
End If
If [not IsEmpty(
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationTitle
)]
Set Variable [
$l
; Value:

( "
Events.StructuredLocation.NewLocationWithTitle
";
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationTitle
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.StructuredLocation.SetRadius
";
$l
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationRadius
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.StructuredLocation.SetGeoLocation
";
$l
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationGeoLat
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationGeoLong
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Alarm.SetStructuredLocation
";
$alarm
;
$l
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Alarm.SetProximity
";
$alarm
;
Events Create Reminder::AlarmProximity
)]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Item.AddAlarm
";
$reminder
;
$alarm
)]

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>

      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.StructuredLocation.SetRadius"; $l; Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationRadius )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.StructuredLocation.SetGeoLocation"; $l; Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationGeoLat; Events Create Reminder::AlarmLocationGeoLong )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Alarm.SetStructuredLocation"; $alarm; $l)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Alarm.SetProximity"; $alarm; Events Create Reminder::AlarmProximity )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Item.AddAlarm"; $reminder; $alarm )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceDayOfWeek.Copy
functions.description: Creates a copy of the day of week object. You can create a copy to use the same day of week setting with a new recurrence rule. This function checks for a license. Created 10th March 2016 , last changed 13th March 2016 Events.Participant.URL - Events.RecurrenceDayOfWeek.DayOfTheWeek 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceDayOfWeek
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence day of week.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceDayOfWeek.DayOfTheWeek
functions.description: The day of the week.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceDayOfWeek
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence day of week.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceDayOfWeek.NewWithDayOfWeek
functions.description: Creates a object with a day of the week. EKRecurrenceDayOfWeek specifies either a simple day of the week, or the nth instance of a particular day of the week, such as the third Tuesday of every month. The week number is only valid when used with monthly or yearly recurrences, since it would be otherwise meaningless. Valid values for dayOfTheWeek are integers 1-7, which correspond to days of the week with Sunday = 1. Valid values for weekNumber portion are (+/-)1-53, where a negative value indicates a value from the end of the range. For example, in a yearly event -1 means last week of the year. -1 in a Monthly recurrence indicates the last week of the month. The value 0 also indicates the weekNumber is irrelevant (every Sunday, etc.). Day-of-week weekNumber values that are out of bounds for the recurrence type will result in an exception when trying to initialize the recurrence. In particular, weekNumber must be zero when passing EKRecurrenceDayOfWeek objects to initialize a weekly recurrence.

parameters.name: dayOfTheWeek
parameters.description: The day of the week.

parameters.name: WeekNumber
parameters.description: The number of the week.

functions.result: Returns day of week reference ID or error.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceEnd.EndDate

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceEnd
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence end.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceEnd.NewWithEndDate
functions.description: Creates a recurrence end with a specific end date. EKRecurrenceEnd is an attribute of EKRecurrenceRule that defines how long the recurrence is scheduled to repeat. The recurrence can be defined either with a number that indicates the total number times it repeats, or with a date, after which it no longer repeats. An event which is set to never end should have its EKRecurrenceEnd set to empty.   If the end of the pattern is defines with a date, the client must pass a valid date. If the end of the pattern is defined as terms of a number of occurrences, the occurrenceCount passed to the initializer must be positive, it cannot be 0.

parameters.name: EndDate
parameters.description: The new end date as timestamp or date.

functions.result: Returns recurrence end ID or error.

examples.label: Create a recurrence rule with end date for a yearly event:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$rule
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.RecurrenceRule.NewWithFrequency
"; "Yearly";"1" ) ]
Set Variable [
$end
; Value:
MBS
( "Events.RecurrenceEnd.NewWithEndDate"; Date ( 12 ; 30 ; 2025 ) ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.RecurrenceRule.SetRecurrenceEnd
";
$rule
;
$end
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Item.AddRecurrenceRule
";
$item
;
$rule
) ]
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$end</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.RecurrenceRule.SetRecurrenceEnd"; $rule; $end ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceEnd.NewWithOccurrenceCount
functions.description: Creates a recurrence end with a maximum occurrence count. EKRecurrenceEnd is an attribute of EKRecurrenceRule that defines how long the recurrence is scheduled to repeat. The recurrence can be defined either with a number that indicates the total number times it repeats, or with a date, after which it no longer repeats. An event which is set to never end should have its EKRecurrenceEnd set to empty.   If the end of the pattern is defines with a date, the client must pass a valid date. If the end of the pattern is defined as terms of a number of occurrences, the occurrenceCount passed to the initializer must be positive, it cannot be 0.

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: The new occurence count.

functions.result: Returns recurrence end ID or error.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.DaysOfTheMonth
functions.description: Queries days of the month. This property is valid for rules whose frequency is monthly, and that were initialized with one or more specific days of the month (not with a day of the week and week of the month). This property can be accessed as an list containing one or more numbers corresponding to the days of the month the event recurs. For all other rules, this property is empty. This property corresponds to BYMONTHDAY in the iCalendar specification.

The property corresponds to BYDAY in the iCalendar specification.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.FirstDayOfTheWeek
functions.description: Queries first day of week number. Recurrence patterns can specify which day of the week should be treated as the first day. Possible values for this property are integers 0 and 1-7, which correspond to days of the week with Sunday = 1. Zero indicates that the property is not set for this recurrence. The first day of the week only affects the way the recurrence is expanded for weekly recurrence patterns with an interval greater than 1. For those types of recurrence patterns, the Calendar framework will set firstDayOfTheWeek to be 2 (Monday). In all other cases, this property will be set to zero. The iCalendar spec stipulates that the default value is Monday if this property is not set.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceRule
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence rule.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.Frequency
functions.description: Queries frequency. This property designates the unit of time used to describe the recurrence pattern. Value is Daily, Weekly, Monthly or Yearly.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceRule
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence rule.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.GetRecurrenceEnd
functions.description: Queries recurrence end. Can be empty.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceRule
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence rule.

functions.result: Returns recurrence end id, empty or error.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.Interval
functions.description: Queries interval. The interval of a rules is an integer value which specifies how often the recurrence rule repeats over the unit of time described by the frequency. For example, if the frequency is weekly, then an interval of 1 means the pattern is repeated every week. A value of 2 indicates it is repeated every other week, 3 means every third week, and so on. The value must be a positive integer; 0 is not a valid value, and nil will be returned if the client attempts to initialize a rule with a negative or zero interval. This function checks for a license. Created 10th March 2016 , last changed 13th March 2016 Events.RecurrenceRule.GetRecurrenceEnd - Events.RecurrenceRule.MonthsOfTheYear 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceRule
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence rule.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.MonthsOfTheYear
functions.description: Queries months of the year. This property is valid for rules whose frequency is yearly. This property can be accessed as an list containing one or more numbers corresponding to the months of the year the event recurs. For all other  EKRecurrenceRules, this property is empty. This property corresponds to BYMONTH in the iCalendar specification.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceRule
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence rule.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.NewWithFrequency
functions.signature: MBS( "Events.RecurrenceRule.NewWithFrequency"; Frequency; Interval; EKRecurrenceEnd { ; daysOfTheWeek; daysOfTheMonth; monthsOfTheYear; weeksOfTheYear; daysOfTheYear; setPositions } )
functions.description: Creates a new recurrence rule. This can be used to build any kind of recurrence rule. But be aware that certain combinations make no sense and will be ignored. For example, if you pass daysOfTheWeek for a daily recurrence, they will be ignored.

parameters.name: Frequency
parameters.description: The frequency. Can be Yearly, Monthly, Daily or Weekly.

parameters.name: Interval
parameters.description: The interval to use. Must be > 0

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceEnd
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence end. Can be empty.

parameters.name: daysOfTheWeek
parameters.description: The days of the week list. An list of EKRecurrenceDayOfWeek object identifiers. Valid for all recurrence types except daily. Ignored otherwise. Corresponds to the BYDAY value in the iCalendar specification. Version 7.1 accepts here also week days, not just RecurrenceDayOfWeek IDs returned from Events.RecurrenceDayOfWeek.NewWithDayOfWeek .

parameters.name: daysOfTheMonth
parameters.description: The days of the month. An list of numbers ([+/-] 1 to 31). Negative numbers infer counting from the end of the month. For example, -1 means the last day of the month. Valid only for monthly recurrences. Ignored otherwise. Corresponds to the BYMONTHDAY value in the iCalendar specification.

parameters.name: monthsOfTheYear
parameters.description: The list of months in the year. An list of numbers (1 to 12). Valid only for yearly recurrences. Ignored otherwise. Corresponds to the BYMONTH value in the iCalendar specification.

parameters.name: weeksOfTheYear
parameters.description: The list of weeks in the year. An list of numbers ([+/1] 1 to 53). Negative numbers infer counting from the end of the year. For example, -1 means the last week of the year. Valid only for yearly recurrences. Ignored otherwise. Corresponds to the BYWEEKNO value in the iCalendar specification.

parameters.name: daysOfTheYear
parameters.description: The days of the year. An list of numbers ([+/1] 1 to 366). Negative numbers infer counting from the end of the year. For example, -1 means the last day of the year. Valid only for yearly recurrences. Ignored otherwise. Corresponds to the BYYEARDAY value in the iCalendar specification.

parameters.name: setPositions
parameters.description: The list of set positions. An list of numbers ([+/1] 1 to 366). Used at the end of recurrence computation to filter the list to the positions specified. Negative numbers indicate starting at the end, i.e. -1 indicates taking the last result of the set. Valid when daysOfTheWeek, daysOfTheMonth, monthsOfTheYear, weeksOfTheYear, or  daysOfTheYear is passed. Ignored otherwise. Corresponds to the BYSETPOS value in the iCalendar specification.

"Monthly"; // by mont

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.SetPositions
functions.description: Queries the set positions. This property is valid for rules which have a valid daysOfTheWeek, daysOfTheMonth, weeksOfTheYear, or monthsOfTheYear property. It allows you to specify a set of ordinal numbers to help choose which objects out of the set of selected events should be included. For example, setting the daysOfTheWeek to Monday-Friday and including a value of -1 in the array would indicate the last weekday in the recurrence range (month, year, etc). This value corresponds to the iCalendar BYSETPOS property.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceRule
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence rule.

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.SetRecurrenceEnd
functions.description: Sets the recurrence end. This property defines when the the repeating event is scheduled to end. The end date can be specified by a number of occurrences, or with an end date.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceRule
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence rule.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceEnd
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence end.

examples.label: Create a recurrence rule with end date for a yearly event:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$rule
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.RecurrenceRule.NewWithFrequency
"; "Yearly";"1" ) ]
Set Variable [
$end
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.RecurrenceEnd.NewWithEndDate
"; Date ( 12 ; 30 ; 2025 ) ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Events.RecurrenceRule.SetRecurrenceEnd";
$rule
;
$end
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Item.AddRecurrenceRule
";
$item
;
$rule
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.RecurrenceRule.NewWithFrequency"; "Yearly";"1" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$rule</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.RecurrenceEnd.NewWithEndDate"; Date ( 12 ; 30 ; 2025 ) ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$end</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.RecurrenceRule.SetRecurrenceEnd"; $rule; $end ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Item.AddRecurrenceRule"; $item; $rule ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.RecurrenceRule.WeeksOfTheYear
functions.description: Queries weeks of the year. This property is valid for rules whose frequency is yearly. This property can be accessed as an list containing one or more numbers corresponding to the weeks of the year the event recurs. For all other rules, this property is empty. This property corresponds to BYWEEK in the iCalendar specification. It should contain integers from 1 to 53 or -1 to -53.

parameters.name: EKRecurrenceRule
parameters.description: The reference to the recurrence rule.

functions.name: Events.RefreshSourcesIfNecessary
functions.description: Cause a sync to potentially occur taking into account the necessity of it. You can call this method to pull new data from remote sources.   This only updates the event store's data.  If you want to update your objects after  refreshing the sources, you should call refresh on each of them afterwards. On iOS, this sync only occurs if deemed necessary.   On OS X, this will occur regardless of necessity, but may change in a future release to match the iOS behavior. On WatchOS, initiating sync is not available. Sync will occur automatically with the paired iOS device.

functions.name: Events.Reminder.SetDueDateComponents
functions.signature: MBS( "Events.Reminder.SetDueDateComponents"; EKReminder { ; Year; Month; Day; Hour; Minute; Second; TimeZone } )
functions.description: The date by which this reminder should be completed. The use of date components allows the due date and its time zone to be represented in a single property.  A undefined time zone represents a floating date.  Setting a date component without a hour, minute and second component will set allDay to true.

parameters.name: EKReminder
parameters.description: The reference to the reminder.

parameters.name: Year
parameters.description: The year number. If missing or negative, no year is defined.

parameters.name: Month
parameters.description: The month number. If missing or negative, no month is defined.

parameters.name: Day
parameters.description: The day number. If missing or negative, no day is defined.

parameters.name: Hour
parameters.description: The hour number. If missing or negative, no hour is defined.

parameters.name: Minute
parameters.description: The minute number. If missing or negative, no minute is defined.

parameters.name: Second
parameters.description: The second number. If missing or negative, no second is defined.

parameters.name: TimeZone
parameters.description: The time zone to use. Value of "System" sets to system time zone. Value of "Default" sets to default time zone.

functions.name: Events.Reminder.SetPriority
functions.description: Sets the priority of the reminder. Priorities run from 1 (highest) to 9 (lowest).  A priority of 0 means no priority. Saving a reminder with any other priority will fail. Per RFC 5545, priorities of 1-4 are considered "high," a priority of 5 is "medium," and priorities of 6-9 are "low."

parameters.name: EKReminder
parameters.description: The reference to the reminder.

parameters.name: Priority
parameters.description: The new priority. Number from 0 to 9. Or texts high, medium, low or none.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.RemoveCalendar
functions.description: Removes a calendar from the database. This function attempts to delete the given calendar from the calendar database. It returns OK if successful and error otherwise. If the calendar supports multiple entity types (allowedEntityTypes), but the user has not granted you access to all those entity types, then we will delete all of the entity types for which you have access and remove that entity type from the allowedEntityTypes. For example: If a calendar supports both events and reminders, but you only have access to reminders, we will delete all the reminders and make the calendar only support events. If you have access to all of its allowedEntityTypes, then it will delete the calendar and all of the events and reminders in the calendar. You may still need to call Events.Commit later.

parameters.name: EKCalendar
parameters.description: The identifier or name of the calendar.

parameters.name: Commit
parameters.description: Whether to commit. Pass 1 to commit or 0 to not commit. Default is 1. Pass 0 to batch changes together and commit with Events.Commit later.

functions.name: Events.RemoveEvent
functions.description: Removes an event from the calendar store. This function attempts to remove the event from the calendar database. It returns OK if successful and error otherwise. It's possible for this function to return failed. This occurs if the event wasn't ever added and didn't need removing. After an event is removed, it is no longer tied to this calendar store, and all data in the event is cleared except for the eventIdentifier. You may still need to call Events.Commit later.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

parameters.name: Span
parameters.description: The span to use. This event, or this and future events. Pass "This" or "Future" here.

parameters.name: Commit
parameters.description: Whether to commit. Pass 1 to commit or 0 to not commit. Default is 1. Pass 0 to batch changes together and commit with Events.Commit later.

examples.label: Remove event:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Events.RemoveEvent"; CalendarTest::ItemUID; "this"; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.RemoveReminder
functions.description: Removes a reminder from the event store. This function attempts to remove the reminder from the event store database. It returns OK if successful and Failed otherwise. After a reminder is removed, it is no longer tied to this event store. You may still need to call Events.Commit later.

parameters.name: EKReminder
parameters.description: The reference to the reminder.

parameters.name: Commit
parameters.description: Whether to commit. Pass 1 to commit or 0 to not commit. Default is 1. Pass 0 to batch changes together and commit with Events.Commit later.

functions.name: Events.RequestAccessToEntityType
functions.description: Requests access for the entity type. Users are able to grant or deny access to event and reminder data on a per-app basis. To request access to event and/or reminder data, call Events.requestAccessToEntityType. This will not block the app while the user is being asked to grant or deny access. Until access has been granted for an entity type, the event store will not contain any calendars for that entity type, and any attempt to save will fail. The user will only be prompted the first time access is requested; any subsequent instantiations of EKEventStore will use the existing permissions. You can later check status with Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType . The function may return OK if access is granted directly or Wait if user will be asked. Please note, that on macOS Sonoma, you may only be allowed to add entries to the calendars unless the user grants full permissions in system preferences.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Which entity type you like to request: Event or Reminder.

examples.label: Request access for reminder:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.requestAccessToEntityType"; "Reminder" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query authorization:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType
"; "Reminder")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["We have a problem";
MBS
("
Text.RemovePrefix
";
$r
; "[
MBS
] ")]
Else If [
$r
= "NotDetermined"]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Events.requestAccessToEntityType"; "Reminder" )]
If [
$r
= "Wait"]
#dialog is showing.
Else If [
$r
= "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Success"; "We are authorized and ready to go."]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["We have a problem";
$r
]
End If
Else If [
$r
= "Restricted"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Success"; "We are authorized for a restricted access and ready to go."]
Else If [
$r
= "Denied"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Problem"; "We are denied for calendar access. Please go to system preferences and enable us."]
Else If [
$r
= "Authorized"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Success"; "We are authorized and ready to go."]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["We have a problem";
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType"; "Reminder")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"We have a problem"; MBS("Text.RemovePrefix"; $r</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"[MBS] ")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$r = "NotDetermined"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.requestAccessToEntityType"; "Reminder" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$r = "Wait"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>dialog is showing.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$r = "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Success"</Calculation>

functions.name: Events.RequestFullAccessToEvents
functions.description: Prompts people to grant or deny read and write access to event data. Requesting access to an event store asynchronously prompts people for permission to use their data. The operating system only prompts them the first time your app requests full access to events; any subsequent instantiations of EKEventStore uses existing permissions. You can later check status with Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType . The function may return OK if access is granted directly or Wait if user will be asked. Your app isn’t blocked while the person decides to grant or deny permission. Because they may deny permission, your app should handle cases where it doesn’t receive access to the event store. Requires macOS 14.0 or iOS 17.0 or newer.

examples.label: Request access for events:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.RequestFullAccessToEvents" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

The operating system only prompts them the first time your app requests access to reminders; any subsequent instantiations of EKEventStore uses existing permissions. You can later check status with Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType . The function may return OK if access is granted directly or Wait if user will be asked. Your app isn’t blocked while the person decides to grant or deny permission. Because they may deny permission, your app should handle cases where it doesn’t receive access to the event store. Requires macOS 14.0 or iOS 17.0 or newer.

functions.name: Events.RequestWriteOnlyAccessToEvents
functions.description: Prompts the person using your app to grant or deny write access to event data. Requesting access to an event store asynchronously prompts people for permission to use their data. The operating system only prompts them the first time your app requests write-only event access; any subsequent instantiations of EKEventStore uses existing permissions. You can later check status with Events.AuthorizationStatusForEntityType . The function may return OK if access is granted directly or Wait if user will be asked. Your app isn’t blocked while the person decides to grant or deny permission. Because they may deny permission, your app should handle cases where it doesn’t receive access to the event store. Requires macOS 14.0 or iOS 17.0 or newer.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.Reset
functions.description: Resets the event store. You can use this method to forget ALL changes made to the event store (all additions, all fetched objects, etc.). It essentially is as if you released the store and then created a new one. It brings it back to its initial state. All objects ever created/fetched, etc. using this store are no longer connected to it and are considered invalid.

functions.name: Events.SaveCalendar
functions.description: Saves changes to a calendar, or adds a new calendar to the database. This functions attempts to save the given calendar to the calendar database. It returns OK if successful and an error otherwise. You may still need to call Events.Commit later.

parameters.name: EKCalendar
parameters.description: The identifier or name of the calendar.

parameters.name: Commit
parameters.description: Whether to commit. Pass 1 to commit or 0 to not commit. Default is 1. Pass 0 to batch changes together and commit with Events.Commit later.

examples.label: Create a new calendar:
examples.text_code:
# We need a source to add the new calendar to. I prefer local, but newer macOS doesn't have it, so we use caldav for iCloud
Set Variable [
$source
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.SourceWithType
"; "local" ) ]
If [ IsEmpty (
$source
) ]
Set Variable [
$source
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.SourceWithType
"; "caldav" ) ]
End If
# New calendar for events
Set Variable [
$calendar
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.NewCalendar
"; "Event" ) ]
# configure it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Calendar.SetTitle
";
$Calendar
; "Test" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Calendar.SetColor
";
$Calendar
; "red" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Calendar.SetSource
";
$Calendar
;
$Source
) ]
# save to database
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Events.SaveCalendar";
$calendar
;1 ) ]
# and check what ID we got for later
Set Variable [
$id
; Value:
MBS
( "
Events.Calendar.CalendarIdentifier
";
$Calendar
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Saved calendar" ;
$r
& ¶ &
$id
]

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$source</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.NewCalendar"; "Event" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$calendar</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> configure it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Calendar.SetTitle"; $Calendar; "Test" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Calendar.SetColor"; $Calendar; "red" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.Calendar.SetSource"; $Calendar; $Source ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save to database</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.SaveCalendar"; $calendar ;1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

functions.name: Events.SaveEvent
functions.description: Saves changes to an event permanently. This function attempts to save the event to the calendar database. It returns OK if successful and error otherwise. It's possible for this function to return failed. This occurs if the event wasn't dirty and didn't need saving. After an event is successfully saved, it is also put into sync with the database, meaning that all fields you did not change will be updated to the latest values. If you save the event, but it was deleted by a different store/process, you will effectively recreate the event as a new event. You may still need to call Events.Commit later.

parameters.name: EKEvent
parameters.description: The reference to the event.

parameters.name: Span
parameters.description: The span to use. This event, or this and future events. Pass "This" or "Future" here.

parameters.name: Commit
parameters.description: Whether to commit. Pass 1 to commit or 0 to not commit. Default is 1. Pass 0 to batch changes together and commit with Events.Commit later.

functions.name: Events.SelectEvent
functions.description: Selects an event via ID. This function takes a calendar ID, either internal or external identifier for events or reminders and finds it. Returns the internal ID. Result is empty if nothing is found.

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: the ID for the calendar entry.

examples.label: Select an event by ID:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.SetNotification
functions.description: Installs a notification script. This script is called whenever something in the calendar database changed. Notification is sent out when the database is changed by either an external process, another event store in the same process, or by calling saveEvent: or removeEvent: on a store you are managing. When you receive this notification, you should consider all objects instances you have to be invalid. If you had selected events for a date range using Events.Events , etc. for display, you should release them and re-fetch the events again. If you have an event you are actively using (e.g. you are currently viewing details for it or editing it), you can call Events.Object.Refresh to try to revalidate it against the latest state of the database. If that method returns 1, you can continue to use the event, otherwise, you should release it and abandon what you were doing with it. This script trigger will also be posted if access to events or reminders is changed by the user.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

examples.label: Installs notification script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Events.SetNotification"; "test.fmp12"; "MyScript" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Events.SetNotificationEvaluate
functions.description: Installs a notification evaluation. This expression is evaluated whenever something in the calendar database changed. Notification is sent out when the database is changed by either an external process, another event store in the same process, or by calling saveEvent: or removeEvent: on a store you are managing. When you receive this notification, you should consider all objects instances you have to be invalid. If you had selected events for a date range using Events.Events , etc. for display, you should release them and re-fetch the events again. If you have an event you are actively using (e.g. you are currently viewing details for it or editing it), you can call Events.Object.Refresh to try to revalidate it against the latest state of the database. If that method returns 1, you can continue to use the event, otherwise, you should release it and abandon what you were doing with it. This expression will also be evaluated if access to events or reminders is changed by the user.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

functions.name: Events.Sources
functions.description: Returns an unordered list of source IDs.

parameters.name: AsJSON
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as JSON.

examples.xml_code:

examples.label: Queries sources and types:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of event sources IDs
	list = MBS( "Events.Sources" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	id = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query name for each ID
	name = MBS( "Events.Source.Title"; id); 
	type = MBS( "Events.Source.Type"; id); 
	names = names & name & " -> " & type & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
