functions.name: ImageView.Clear
functions.description: Clears image.

parameters.name: ImageView
parameters.description: The image view reference number.

functions.name: ImageView.CreateWithControl
functions.description: Creates a new image view on the given window covering the area of the given control. Use ImageView.Release later to destroy the image view. If you use a register (tab controls) on the window, be aware that the image view actually is in front of the register (tab controls). So you need to check in the script which register page is current and should show what image view. Or by a trigger you turn the visibility on and off. Image view reference numbers are starting at 74000 and counting up for each new image view. If you create a control on a new window, you may do window update command in your script first to have FileMaker actually build the window with controls and display it. A short script pause or using FM.RunScriptLater with 0.1 delay can help having all controls draw themselves. Then you can use this command to put our control on top. We use GetLayoutObjectAttribute() to query left, top, width and height and this gives empty value if control is not yet here. Creation fails to pick the right window in case the window is selected by name and the window name is not unique. In that case better work with window reference numbers or just pass 0 for current front window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to top or bottom.

functions.result: Returns image view reference number or error.

examples.label: Create image view with control as placeholder:
examples.text_code:
# Query size of image
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
ImageView::ImageContainer
) ]
Set Variable [
$w
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.GetWidth
";
$Image
) ]
Set Variable [
$h
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.GetHeight
";
$Image
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]
# We play half size for retina screen resolution
Set Variable [
$h
; Value: Round(
$h
/2;0) ]
Set Variable [
$w
; Value: Round(
$w
/2;0) ]
# We play half size for retina screen resolution
Set Variable [
$$ImageView
; Value:
MBS
("ImageView.CreateWithControl"; 0; "PlaceHolder") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ImageView.SetImage
";
$$ImageView
;
ImageView::ImageContainer
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query size of image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; ImageView::ImageContainer ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.GetWidth"; $Image ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$w</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.GetHeight"; $Image ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$h</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMimage.Release"; $Image ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> We play half size for retina screen resolution</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Round($h/2;0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$h</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Round($w/2;0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$w</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> We play half size for retina screen resolution</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageView.CreateWithControl"; 0; "PlaceHolder") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$ImageView</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageView.SetImage"; $$ImageView; ImageView::ImageContainer) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImageView.CreateWithSize
functions.signature: MBS( "ImageView.CreateWithSize"; WindowRef; X; Y; W; H )
functions.description: Creates a new image view inside the window with the given rectangle. Use ImageView.Release later to destroy the image view. If you use a register (tab controls) on the window, be aware that the image view actually is in front of the register (tab controls). So you need to check in the script which register page is current and should show what image view. Or by a trigger you turn the visibility on and off. Image view reference numbers are starting at 74000 and counting up for each new image view.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The left coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The top coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: W
parameters.description: The width of the image view in the window.

parameters.name: H
parameters.description: The height of the image view in the window.

functions.result: Returns image view reference number or error.

functions.name: ImageView.CreateWithWindow
functions.description: Creates a new image view which covers the whole window. Use ImageView.Release later to destroy the image view. If you use a register (tab controls) on the window, be aware that the image view actually is in front of the register (tab controls). So you need to check in the script which register page is current and should show what image view. Or by a trigger you turn the visibility on and off. Image view reference numbers are starting at 74000 and counting up for each new image view.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.result: Returns image view reference number or error.

functions.name: ImageView.SetFrame

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The x coordinate.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The y coordinate.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width of the control.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the control.

functions.name: ImageView.SetFrameWithControl
functions.description: Repositions image view to given control. If you moved control in layout mode, this can move the drop area back to the new position. Also useful to switch between different locations on the layout.

parameters.name: ImageView
parameters.description: The image view reference number.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to top or bottom.

functions.name: ImageView.SetImage
functions.description: Sets the image to be shown in the image view. We treat GIF images special and animate them.

parameters.name: ImageView
parameters.description: The image view reference number.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: the image to use. For best results, pass a container value with a PNG image. For animation, pass a GIF image.

  </Step>

functions.name: ImageView.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the tag string for this image view. Use the tag to store whatever value you want to associate with the image view. You can later query it using ImageView.GetTag .

parameters.name: ImageView

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: A string you want to associate with the image view.

functions.name: ImageView.SetToolTip
functions.description: Sets tooltip for image view. The tooltip is visible on Mac when mouse stays over control. There are no tooltips for iOS.

parameters.name: ImageView

parameters.name: Tooltip
parameters.description: The new help text.

functions.name: ImageView.SetVisible
functions.description: Hides or shows the image view. By default we create it visible, but you can hide it if it's on a register and the user clicked to other tab.

parameters.name: ImageView
parameters.description: The image view reference number.

parameters.name: Visible
parameters.description: The new visible state.

functions.name: JavaScript.Available
functions.description: Checks whether the JavaScriptCore library is loaded. Always loaded on macOS since we use the one coming with macOS. For Windows you can load JavaScriptCore.dll and for Linux you can load the JavaScriptCore.so file with JavaScript.LoadLibrary function. Returns always 1 on macOS and iOS.

examples.label: Check status:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "JavaScript.Available" ) = 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "JavaScript library loaded." ]
Else
# on Windows and Linux, you must load it via script
Show Custom Dialog [ "JavaScript library not loaded." ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "JavaScript.Available" ) = 1 ] 
	Show Custom Dialog [ "JavaScript library loaded." ] 
Else
	# on Windows and Linux, you must load it via script
	Show Custom Dialog [ "JavaScript library not loaded." ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JavaScript.CF
functions.description: Calls a global function given the name with FileMaker values. Please initialize the global "js" JavaScript environment before (see JavaScript.New ) and fill it with function definitions as needed (see JavaScript.RegisterFunction ). This may include functions to do FileMaker evaluate (see JavaScript.RegisterEvaluateFunction ), SQL execute (see JavaScript.RegisterSQLQueryFunction ) and starting scripts (see JavaScript.RegisterRunScriptFunction ). Parameters are passed to the function. Please pass as many parameters in FileMaker as needed. Numbers are passed as number and other values are passed as text. If the function needs more parameter, undefined values are used to fill up. If the function needs less parameters, additional ones are dropped. There is no limit in the number of parameters for MBS Plugin here. Returns the result of the function converted to FileMaker, e.g. as text or number value. Same as JavaScript.CallFunction , but takes "js" as the global JavaScript engine. See our example for JavaScript Custom Functions. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Parameters parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the function.

parameters.name: Parameters...
parameters.description: Value to pass as parameter.

functions.name: JavaScript.CallFunction
functions.description: Calls a global function given the name with FileMaker values. Parameters are passed to the function. Please pass as many parameters in FileMaker as needed. Numbers are passed as number and other values are passed as text. If the function needs more parameter, undefined values are used to fill up. If the function needs less parameters, additional ones are dropped. Returns the result of the function converted to FileMaker, e.g. as text or number value. If you like to use JSON instead, please put in properties or pass JSON as string and let JavaScript decode it as part of the function. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Parameters parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: JavaScriptContext
parameters.description: The reference number of the javascript context.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the function.

parameters.name: Parameters...
parameters.description: Value to pass as parameter.

functions.name: JavaScript.CheckScriptSyntax
functions.description: Checks script syntax. Returns OK on success or error message.

parameters.name: JavaScriptContext
parameters.description: The reference number of the javascript context.

parameters.name: Script
parameters.description: The script to evaluate.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The source URL for this script. A URL for the script's source file. Used by debuggers and when reporting exceptions. This parameter is informative only: it does not change the behavior of the script.

parameters.name: startingLineNumber
parameters.description: An integer value specifying the script's starting line number in the file located at sourceURL. This is only used when reporting exceptions. The value is one-based, so the first line is line 1 and invalid values are clamped to 1.

functions.name: JavaScript.EvaluateScript
functions.description: Evaluate a string of JavaScript code. See also WebView.RunJavaScript or WebView.Evaluate for use with web viewer. See also JS.Evaluate and JS.EvaluateToString to run JavaScript cross-platform without web viewer. The evaluate runs within the FileMaker process. If you cause an endless loop, FileMaker freezes and needs to be force quit, so please always include an exit condition for your loops. Also if you get JavaScript to crash, FileMaker will crash with it.

parameters.name: JavaScriptContext
parameters.description: The reference number of the javascript context.

parameters.name: Script
parameters.description: The script to evaluate.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The source URL for this script. A URL for the script's source file. Used by debuggers and when reporting exceptions. This parameter is informative only: it does not change the behavior of the script.

parameters.name: startingLineNumber
parameters.description: An integer value specifying the script's starting line number in the file located at sourceURL. This is only used when reporting exceptions. The value is one-based, so the first line is line 1 and invalid values are clamped to 1.

examples.label: Defines and runs a function in JavaScript:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
f = "
function makeCRCTable(){
    var c;
    var crcTable = [];
    for(var n =0; n < 256; n++){
        c = n;
        for(var k =0; k < 8; k++){
            c = ((c&1) ? (0xEDB88320 ^ (c >>> 1)) : (c >>> 1));
        }
        crcTable[n] = c;
    }
    return crcTable;
}

function crc32(str) {
    var crcTable = crcTable || (crcTable = makeCRCTable());
    var crc = 0 ^ (-1);

    for (var i = 0; i < str.length; i++ ) {
        crc = (crc >>> 8) ^ crcTable[(crc ^ str.charCodeAt(i)) & 0xFF];
    }

    return (crc ^ (-1)) >>> 0;
};

crc32(InputValue);";
j = MBS( "JavaScript.New" );
r = MBS( "JavaScript.SetProperty"; j; "InputValue"; "Hello World" );
r = MBS( "JavaScript.EvaluateScript"; j; f );
x = MBS( "JavaScript.Release"; j )
];r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JavaScript.LoadLibrary
functions.description: Loads the JavaScriptCore library. Does nothing on macOS or iOS since we use the library provided by Apple with the operating system. You can load JavaScriptCore.dll on Windows here. Make sure all dependencies are honored and you may use Dependency Walker application to look for dependencies. You can load libjavascriptcoregtk.so on Linux here. e.g. "/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjavascriptcoregtk-4.1.so" for Linux 64-bit on ARM. On Linux you need to install a package which includes JavaScriptCore library, e.g. using "sudo apt-get install libjavascriptcoregtk-4.1" command. Returns OK on success and an error message otherwise. If you have trouble with DLLs not loading on Windows, please check if you have new Visual Studio Runtime libraries installed. If you need more than one DLL, please use Process.SetDllDirectory to point to the folder to find the DLL files. On Linux if you get the error wrong ELF class: ELFCLASS32 if you try to load a 32-bit library into a 64-bit application. You may also get errors about version GLIBC_2.25 or similar if your libc library on the Linux installation isn't new enough for the loaded library.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the library.

examples.label: Load library on Linux:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JavaScript.LoadLibrary"; "/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjavascriptcoregtk-4.1.so") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

("JavaScript.New") ]

("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Failed to initialize"; Message:

; Default Button: “OK” ]

$$Context
; Value:
$r
]
Show Custom Dialog [ Message: "OK"; Default Button: “OK” ]

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JavaScript.Release
";
$js
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JavaScript.New" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$js</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JavaScript.RegisterEvaluateFunction"; $js; "FMEvaluate" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JavaScript.EvaluateScript"; $js; "FMEvaluate('Get(AccountName)') + ' from javascript'") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Result" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JavaScript.Release"; $js ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JavaScript.RegisterFunction
functions.signature: MBS( "JavaScript.RegisterFunction"; JavaScriptContext; FunctionName { ; ParameterNames; Body; SourceURL; startingLineNumber } )
functions.description: Registers a javascript function.

parameters.name: JavaScriptContext
parameters.description: The reference number of the javascript context.

parameters.name: FunctionName
parameters.description: The name for the new function.

parameters.name: ParameterNames
parameters.description: The names for the parameters.

parameters.name: Body
parameters.description: The body of the function.

parameters.name: SourceURL
parameters.description: A text containing a URL for the script's source file. This is only used when reporting exceptions. Pass empty text if you do not care to include source file information in exceptions.

parameters.name: startingLineNumber
parameters.description: An integer value specifying the script's starting line number in the file located at sourceURL. This is only used when reporting exceptions. The value is one-based, so the first line is line 1 and invalid values are clamped to 1.

examples.label: Register a function:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "JavaScript.RegisterFunction";
$$Context
; "Test"; "Value¶Count"; "return Value * Count;")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JavaScript.RegisterFunction"; $$Context; "Test"; "Value¶Count"; "return Value * Count;")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JavaScript.RegisterRunScriptFunction
functions.description: Registers a run script function. The javascript function takes first parameter the filename, second parameter the script name. Optionally third parameter is passed as text to the script. Call for example like this: FMRunScript('Javascript.fp7', 'test', 'Hello World')

parameters.name: JavaScriptContext
parameters.description: The reference number of the javascript context.

parameters.name: FunctionName
parameters.description: The name for the new function.

examples.label: Register RunScript function:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "JavaScript.RegisterRunScriptFunction";
$$Context
; "FMRunScript" )]
# later:
# FMRunScript('Javascript.fp7', 'test', 'Hello World')
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JavaScript.RegisterRunScriptFunction"; $$Context; "FMRunScript" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> later:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> FMRunScript('Javascript.fp7', 'test', 'Hello World')</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JavaScript.RegisterSQLQueryFunction
functions.description: Registers a SQLQuery function. The javascript function has as parameter first the SQL expression. Second optional parameter is the file name to limit query to only this FileMaker database file. Third and forth parameter are separators for column or row which can be text or number. Default is column = tab and row = return. Fifth and following parameters are passed as parameters to SQL. Call like this: FMQuery('select * from Hello') or FMQuery('select * from Hello where "Count"=?', 'JavaScript.fmp12', 9, 13, 2)

parameters.name: JavaScriptContext
parameters.description: The reference number of the javascript context.

parameters.name: FunctionName
parameters.description: The name for the new function.

examples.label: Register query function:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JavaScript.ReleaseAll

functions.name: List.AddPostfix
functions.description: Adds a postfix text to each entry in the list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Postfix
parameters.description: The text to append.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Add postfix to list:

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.AddPrefix
functions.description: Adds a prefix text to each entry in the list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The text to prepend.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

functions.name: List.AddValue
functions.description: Adds a value to the list. For big lists, using QuickList functions is much faster. The list is only parsed once and stored in memory.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to add.

examples.label: Adds a value to a list:

MBS
( "List.AddValue"; "Hello¶World"; "Third" )

Hello
World

examples.xml_code:

functions.name: List.And
functions.description: Returns a list with values which are contained in both lists. Performs a logical AND operation. This function is made for lists without duplicate values as it checks if values from one list exists in others, which may not work correctly if one list has duplicate values.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Perform logical AND on list:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.AND"; "1¶2¶3¶4"; "3¶4¶5¶6" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.AndColumn
functions.signature: MBS( "List.AndColumn"; List1; List2; Separator; Column { ; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Returns a list with values which are contained in both lists. Performs a logical AND operation. Returns a list with values from list2 where the columns compared are contained in both lists. Checks the given column in both lists too compare. If Separator is empty, this is same as List.And , but slower.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: AND two lists based on 2 column:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.AndColumn"; 
"1 test 3¶a b c¶d¶e f"; 
"2 test 4¶a f c¶d b"; 
" "; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.BestMatch
functions.description: Looks for best match of a text in a list of texts. If not found, returns empty result, so FileMaker function isEmpty() will tell you if it's empty (instead of just having no text). Uses internally the Text.LevenshteinDistance function to compare all items and look for best match. If no entry matches over 50%, this may return empty text. See also QuickList.BestMatch in case you need to query several times and want to load list only once.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: SearchText
parameters.description: The text to search.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 0 to use Levenshtein distance. Pass 1 to use Jaro-Winkler distance. Add 2 to return list of top 5 entries.

functions.result: Returns best match, empty or error.

examples.label: Looking for best match for Hilo will find Hello:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.BestMatch"; "Test¶Hello¶World¶Hallo"; "Hilo" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.CSVSplit
functions.description: Splits CSV text. Plugin automatically detects if comma or semicolon is used. Returns list with all values. Version 8 can detect tab character, too. Please note that the parsing can handle multi line values, but that causes the list to have multiple entries for the value. Use Matrix.CSVSplit or QuickList.CSVSplit to handle that better as those can hold multi line values.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The CSV text.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter to use. If empty, we auto detect it.

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.CrossProduct
functions.signature: MBS( "List.CrossProduct"; List1; List2 { ; Prefix; Midfix; Postfix; Flags } )
functions.description: Creates the cross product from two lists. Optionally adds prefix, postfix and midfix. Returns empty list if one of the lists are empty!

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list to process.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list to process.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to use

parameters.name: Midfix
parameters.description: The middle to use. This text is put between texts from both lists.

parameters.name: Postfix
parameters.description: The postfix to use

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists. Add 2 for a diagonal cross, so we combine each element of first list with the same element index from the second list.

examples.label: Test with all parameters:

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: List.DeCombine
functions.signature: MBS( "List.DeCombine"; List; ColumnDelimiter; StartColumn; EndColumn { ; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Picks elements from each line in a list. This function combines values from a list by picking only given columns.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: ColumnDelimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter between colums. This is also used in result.

parameters.name: StartColumn
parameters.description: Index of column to start. First is 0.

parameters.name: EndColumn
parameters.description: Index of column to end. First is 0. If you only want one column, please pass same value for start and end column.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.DeleteColumn
functions.description: Deletes a column from each line in a list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Removes second column with index 1:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.DeleteColumn"; "a,b,c¶1,2,3"; ","; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.Deserialize
functions.description: Restores a serialized list.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The serialized data as text.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Save and restore a list:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:"Hello¶World¶1234¶Just a test!"]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("
List.Serialize
";
$list
)]
Show Custom Dialog ["List"; "Encoded: " &
$text
& ¶ & ¶ & "Decoded: " &
MBS
("List.Deserialize";
$text
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Hello¶World¶1234¶Just a test!"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("List.Serialize"; $list)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"List"; "Encoded: " & $text & ¶ & ¶ & "Decoded: " & MBS("List.Deserialize"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$text)</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.Find
functions.description: Finds an entry in the list. Returns -1 if item is not found. The index is zero based.

parameters.name: List

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to look for.

parameters.name: ignoreCase
parameters.description: Boolean to define whether to ignore case. Default is 0 for case sensitive compare. Pass 1 for case insensitive compare.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Look for world:

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: List.FindDuplicateItems
functions.description: Finds duplicate items in the list. Returns list with all duplicate items, but only counts each one once. Similar to List.RemoveDuplicateItems and see also QuickList.FindDuplicateItems .

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

parameters.name: CaseInsensitive
parameters.description: Whether to perform comparison case insensitve. By default we do case sensitive operations, which are faster. Pass 1 for insensitve or 0 for sensitive.

examples.label: Find duplicates
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find duplicates case insensitive:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "List.FindDuplicateItems"; "a¶A¶b¶c¶C"; 0; 1)
Example result:
a
b
c
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.FindDuplicateItems"; "a¶A¶b¶c¶C"; 0; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.FindEquals
functions.description: Finds equal items in both lists. Same as List.And function. This function is made for lists without duplicate values as it checks if values from one list exists in others, which may not work correctly if one list has duplicate values.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Find equal entries in both lists:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.FindEquals"; "Hello" ¶ "World"; "Hello" ¶ "Friends" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.FindUnequals

parameters.name: List1

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

parameters.name: NotFromSecondList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to only returns items from first list not present in second list. Default is 0 to get items from both lists.

examples.label: Find non equal entries in both lists:

    </Repetition>

examples.label: Find items in first list which are not in second list:

functions.name: List.GetValue

functions.name: List.HasDuplicateItems
functions.description: Checks if list has duplicates. Returns 1 if duplicate lines are found or 0 if not.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: CaseInsensitive
parameters.description: Whether to perform comparison case insensitve. By default we do case sensitive operations, which are faster. Pass 1 for insensitve or 0 for sensitive.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

functions.name: List.HasValue
functions.description: Checks whether a list contains a given value. Returns 1 if value is found or 0 if not. Text comparison is case sensitive.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to check.

examples.label: Check if value is in list:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Print what user selected:
examples.text_code:
# Show dialog to select something
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.ClearList
")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.SetPrompt
"; "Please select items to print:")]
# We use tags as the visible titles may be localized.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.AddItemToList
"; "Werbung¶Angebot¶Rechnung¶Erinnerung"; "Flyer¶Offer¶Invoice¶Reminder")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.SetAllowMultipleSelection
"; 1)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.SetSelectedTitle
"; "Offer")]
# Show dialog
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.ShowDialog
")]
# now do things
Set Variable [
$selected
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.GetSelectedTag
")]
If [
MBS
("List.HasValue";
$selected
; "Flyer")]
# print flyer
End If
If [
MBS
("List.HasValue";
$selected
; "Invoice")]
# print invoice
End If
If [
MBS
("List.HasValue";
$selected
; "Offer")]
# print offer
End If
If [
MBS
("List.HasValue";
$selected
; "Reminder")]
# print reminder
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Show dialog to select something</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.ClearList")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.SetPrompt"; "Please select items to print:")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> We use tags as the visible titles may be localized.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.AddItemToList"; "Werbung¶Angebot¶Rechnung¶Erinnerung"; "Flyer¶Offer¶Invoice¶Reminder")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.SetAllowMultipleSelection"; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.SetSelectedTitle"; "Offer")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Show dialog</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.ShowDialog")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now do things</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.GetSelectedTag")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$selected</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("List.HasValue"; $selected; "Flyer")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> print flyer</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("List.HasValue"; $selected; "Invoice")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> print invoice</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("List.HasValue"; $selected; "Offer")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> print offer</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("List.HasValue"; $selected; "Reminder")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> print reminder</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.IndexOfFirstValueMatching
functions.signature: MBS( "List.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; List; Operator; Value { ; Mode; StartRow } )
functions.description: Finds index of first value matching value. This uses text comparison!

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Operator
parameters.description: The compare operator. Can be =, !=, <, >, <=, >=, ≤, ≥, ≠, Prefix or Postfix.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to compare.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Pass 1 for numeric comparison. Default is 0 for text comparison.

parameters.name: StartRow
parameters.description: The row to start at. Default is 0.

examples.label: Find the value =2:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; "1¶2¶3¶4"; "="; 2 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find the value ≥3
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; "1¶2¶3¶4"; ">="; 3 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find hello as prefix:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; "test¶hello_123¶audi 34"; "prefix"; "hello" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find next one in a list:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
	[
		// you have a list
		list = "132430¶132433¶132437¶132445¶132447¶132450¶132463¶132464¶132465¶132466¶132468¶132469¶132470¶";

		// find a value
		index = MBS( "List.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; list; "="; 132450; 1);

		// now pick one after it:
		nextValue = MBS( "List.GetValue"; List; index + 1)
	];
	nextValue
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.Intersection
functions.description: Returns a list with values which are contained in all lists. Performs a logical AND operation. Same as List.And , but with variable number of arguments. The returned list ends with a newline character. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Lists parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: Lists...
parameters.description: The second list or more lists.

examples.label: Intersect three lists:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("List.Intersection"; "A"; "B¶A"; "A¶C")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.MapEntries
functions.description: Maps selection of keys from a key list to value list. Keys don't need to be unique.

parameters.name: Keys
parameters.description: The list of keys.

parameters.name: KeysSelected
parameters.description: The keys you got from the list of keys.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The list of values to query. Should have same size as keys list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.MatchesPostfix

parameters.name: List

parameters.name: Text

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Remove DS_Store files from file list:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("List.XOr"; MBS( "List.MatchesPostfix"; $list; ".DS_Store"); $list)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.MatchesPrefix
functions.description: Finds list entries which have a given prefix text. Comparison is case insensitive.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of texts to search.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to find.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Finds matching prefixes:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.MatchesPrefix"; "Hello¶www¶World"; "w" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.MatchesSubString
functions.description: Finds list entries which contains a given text. Comparison is case insensitive.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to find.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Add 4 to reverse operation (NOT).

examples.label: Try the function:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value: "1234¶5678¶12¶34¶5432¶0123¶3" ]
Set Variable [
$n
; Value:
MBS
( "List.MatchesSubString";
$r
; "12" ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Result" ;
$n
]
# shows 1234 12 0123
Set Variable [
$d
; Value:
MBS
( "List.MatchesSubString";
$r
; "12"; 4 ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Result" ;
$d
]
# shows 5678 34 5432
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"1234¶5678¶12¶34¶5432¶0123¶3" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.MatchesSubString"; $r; "12" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$n</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Result" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$n </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> shows 1234 12 0123</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.MatchesSubString"; $r; "12"; 4 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$d</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Result" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$d </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> shows 5678 34 5432</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Get list of all fields from other tables in the current layout:

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.Not
functions.description: Returns values of first list which are not in second list. Performs a logical NOT operation.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Operate not on two lists:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("List.Not"; "Abc¶Def¶Ghi¶Jkl"; "Abc¶Jkl¶Zui¶Edf" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.NotColumn
functions.signature: MBS( "List.NotColumn"; List1; List2; Separator; Column { ; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Returns values of first list which are not in second list. Performs a logical NOT operation. Checks the given column in both lists too compare. If Separator is empty, this is same as List.Not , but slower.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

functions.name: List.Or
functions.description: Returns values of both lists. Performs a logical OR operation.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Perform logical OR on list:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.Or"; "1¶2¶3¶4"; "3¶4¶5¶6" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.OrColumn
functions.signature: MBS( "List.OrColumn"; List1; List2; Separator; Column { ; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Returns values of both lists. Performs a logical OR operation. Checks the given column in both lists too compare. If Separator is empty, this is same as List.Or , but slower.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Trying OR based on second column:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "List.OrColumn"; "1 test 3¶a b c¶d¶e f"; "2 test 4¶a f c¶d b"; " "; 1 )
Example result:
1 test 3
a b c
d
e f
2 test 4
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.OrColumn"; "1 test 3¶a b c¶d¶e f"; "2 test 4¶a f c¶d b"; " "; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.RegExMatch
functions.signature: MBS( "List.RegExMatch"; List; Pattern; CompilerOptions; ExecuteOptions { ; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Matches a list against a pattern. This compiles the pattern, executes it and looks if we have a match or not for each list item. Returns the list items which match.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of texts to search.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to use.

parameters.name: CompilerOptions
parameters.description: A number for the options to use. e.g. 1 for case insensitive or 512 for ungreedy.

parameters.name: ExecuteOptions
parameters.description: The options for executing.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Match Hello with pattern:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.RegExMatch"; "Hello¶Test¶World¶House¶Test"; "H(.*)o")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: same without return on end of result:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.RegExMatch"; "Hello¶Test¶World¶House¶Test"; "H(.*)o”; 0; 0; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.Remove
functions.description: Removes the value with the given index in the list. Returns a new list where the value is missing. For big lists, using QuickList functions is much faster. The list is only parsed once and stored in memory.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the item to remove. Range from 0 to QuickList.Count -1.

examples.label: Remove an entry:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.Remove"; "Hello¶World¶Test"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.RemoveDuplicateItems
functions.description: Removes duplicate items in the list. Similar to UniqueValues function in FileMaker to find unique values. See also QuickList.RemoveDuplicateItems function.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

parameters.name: RemoveAll
parameters.description: If duplicate is found also remove the first occurrence. Set to 1 to enable or 0 to disable. Default is disabled.

parameters.name: CaseInsensitive
parameters.description: Whether to perform comparison case insensitve. By default we do case sensitive operations, which are faster. Pass 1 for insensitve or 0 for sensitive.

examples.label: Remove duplicates:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.RemoveDuplicateItems"; "Hello¶World¶Hello" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Remove all duplicates
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.RemoveDuplicateItems"; "Hello¶World¶Hello"; 0; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.RemoveEmptyItems
functions.description: Removes empty items in the list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Remove empty lines:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.RemovePostfix
functions.description: Removes a postfix text to each entry in the list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Postfix
parameters.description: The text to remove.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

parameters.name: CaseSensitive
parameters.description: Whether to match with case sensitive comparison. Default is 0 for case insensitive. You can pass 1 to have a case sensitive comparison.

examples.label: Remove postfix from list:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.RemovePostfix"; "Hello?¶Other¶World?"; "?")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.RemovePrefix
functions.description: Removes a prefix text to each entry in the list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The text to remove.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

parameters.name: CaseSensitive
parameters.description: Whether to match with case sensitive comparison. Default is 0 for case insensitive. You can pass 1 to have a case sensitive comparison.

examples.label: Remove prefix from list:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.RemoveValue
functions.description: Removes a value from a list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to remove.

parameters.name: All
parameters.description: Optional, pass 1 to remove all values with the given value. Pass 0 (default) to remove only the first matching value.

functions.name: List.RemoveValues

functions.name: List.Reverse

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

functions.name: List.Serialize
functions.description: Serialized a list as a text. The dictionary content is stored in a base64 encoded string which the plugin function List.Deserialize and QuickList.Deserialize can read. Using this function allows to store a list in a text field or transmit it with Socket functions, SerialPort functions or by writing it to a file.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

examples.label: Save and restore a list:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:"Hello¶World¶1234¶Just a test!"]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("List.Serialize";
$list
)]
Show Custom Dialog ["List"; "Encoded: " &
$text
& ¶ & ¶ & "Decoded: " &
MBS
("
List.Deserialize
";
$text
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Hello¶World¶1234¶Just a test!"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("List.Serialize"; $list)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"List"; "Encoded: " & $text & ¶ & ¶ & "Decoded: " & MBS("List.Deserialize"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$text)</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.SetValue
functions.description: Sets a value in the list. If index is equal to count, we append the text to the list. For big lists, using QuickList functions is much faster. The list is only parsed once and stored in memory.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to process.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the value to set. Must be in range from 0 to ValueCount(List).

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to add.

examples.label: Replaces second value with a new text:
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.Shuffle
functions.description: Shuffles the items in the list. We use random number generator to swap items randomly and return a new list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to use

examples.label: Shuffle a list of values:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "List.Shuffle"; "a¶b¶c¶d¶e¶f" )

c
d
e
b
f
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.Shuffle"; "a¶b¶c¶d¶e¶f" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.Sort
functions.description: Sorts the list. Version 6.2 adds new modes for date sorting. Mode 4 for DD.MM.YYYY style and mode 8 for MM.DD.YYYY style. Version 7.5 adds number sorting.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to sort.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Optional, whether to sort case insensitive or descending. Pass 1 to sort case insensitive or 0 to sort case sensitive. Add 2 to sort descending. Add 4 for sorting dates in DD.MM.YYYY style and 8 for sorting dates in MM.DD.YYYY style. Add 16 for number sorting (new in 7.5).

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Sort case sensitive:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.Sort"; "Hello¶World¶Test" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Sort case insensitive:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.Sort"; "Hello¶World¶test"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

functions.name: List.SortWithEvaluate
functions.description: Sorts a list with using an expression to evaluate. The expression is run every time two values are compared, so keep it quick. It should return 1 if leftValue is smaller than rightValue. Our normal List.Sort is faster due to not having the overhead of evaluating expressions.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list to sort.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can include "leftValue" and "rightValue" as variables.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Sort numbers numeric with GetAsNumber() function:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("List.SortWithEvaluate"; "1¶22¶3"; "GetAsNumber(leftValue) < GetAsNumber(rightValue)")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Sort dates with using GetAsDate:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("List.SortWithEvaluate"; "1/1/2000¶2/22/2020¶3/4/1998"; "GetAsDate(leftValue) < GetAsDate(rightValue)")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding

functions.name: List.XOr
functions.description: Returns list with values found in either first or second list, but not in both. Performs an exclusive logical OR operation.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Perform logical XOR on list:
examples.xml_code:

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: List.XOrColumn
functions.signature: MBS( "List.XOrColumn"; List1; List2; Separator; Column { ; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Returns list with values found in either first or second list, but not in both. Performs an exclusive logical OR operation. Checks the given column in both lists too compare. If Separator is empty, this is same as List.XOr , but slower.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The second list.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

functions.name: Math.Average
functions.description: Calculates average of given values. Takes values from arguments, which may be list of values or single values. Returns empty result if no values are passed. Uses FileMaker's math functions, so very precise in results. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Values parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Values...
parameters.description: Pass an argument for each value or list of values.

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  </Step>

functions.name: Math.BitwiseAND
functions.description: Perform bitwise AND operation. Only for integer numbers. You can pass several values to combine more than 2 values. Updated to use 64-bit signed integers in version 7.3. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat value2 parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Value1
parameters.description: The first number.

parameters.name: value2...
parameters.description: The second number.

examples.label: Applies bitwise and for three numbers which gives 16:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Math.BitwiseAND"; 255; 48; 18)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Split MIDI Timestamp:
examples.text_code:
# last five bits are frame counter
Set Variable [
$Frame
;
MBS
("Math.BitwiseAND"; TimeStamp; 31)]
# Seconds are in 2nd byte, so take 6 bits after shifting 8 to right
Set Variable [
$Second
;
MBS
( "Math.BitwiseAND";
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseShiftRight
"; TimeStamp; 8 ); 63) ]
# Minutes are in 3rd byte, so take 6 bits after shifting 16 to right
Set Variable [
$Minute
;
MBS
( "Math.BitwiseAND";
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseShiftRight
"; TimeStamp; 16 ); 63) ]
# Hours are in 4th byte: take 5 bits after shifting 24 to right
Set Variable [
$Hour
;
MBS
( "Math.BitwiseAND";
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseShiftRight
"; TimeStamp; 24 ); 31) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> last five bits are frame counter</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Math.BitwiseAND"; TimeStamp; 31)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Frame</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Seconds are in 2nd byte, so take 6 bits after shifting 8 to right</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAND"; MBS( "Math.BitwiseShiftRight"; TimeStamp; 8 ); 63) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Second</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Minutes are in 3rd byte, so take 6 bits after shifting 16 to right</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAND"; MBS( "Math.BitwiseShiftRight"; TimeStamp; 16 ); 63) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Minute</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Hours are in 4th byte: take 5 bits after shifting 24 to right</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAND"; MBS( "Math.BitwiseShiftRight"; TimeStamp; 24 ); 31) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Hour</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Try function:
examples.text_code:
Let([
v1 = 16;
r =
MBS
("
Math.GetBit
"; v1; 4);
v2 =
MBS
("
Math.SetBit
"; v1; 5);
v3 =
MBS
("Math.ClearBit"; v2; 5)
];
"Value before: " & v1 & ¶ &
"Bit 4 is set? " & r & ¶ &
"Value with bit 5 set: " & v2 & ¶ &
"Value with bit 5 cleared: " & v3)
Example result:
Value before: 16
Bit 4 is set? 1
Value with bit 5 set: 48
Value with bit 5 cleared: 16
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([

v1 = 16;
r = MBS("Math.GetBit"; v1; 4);
v2 = MBS("Math.SetBit"; v1; 5);
v3 = MBS("Math.ClearBit"; v2; 5)

]; 
"Value before: " & v1 & ¶ & 
"Bit 4 is set? " & r & ¶ & 
"Value with bit 5 set: " & v2 & ¶ & 
"Value with bit 5 cleared: " & v3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Math.DecodeNumber
functions.description: Decodes a number from it's binary representation. This may be useful for binary protocols with serial ports or sockets.

parameters.name: NumberType
parameters.description: Which type of number to encode. Can be "Float", "Integer" or "UnsignedInteger".

parameters.name: Bits
parameters.description: The number of bits. Can be 32 or 64 for float. Can be 8, 16, 24, 32 or 64 for integers.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The hex encoded binary data.

parameters.name: BigEndian
parameters.description: Pass 1 to encode big endian. Default is 0 for little endian.

examples.label: Decodes a 32-bit floating point number:

    </Value>

    </Repetition>

</fmxmlsnippet>
