functions.name: Math.EncodeNumber
functions.description: Encodes a number as binary data as hex text. You can use Math.DecodeNumber to decode it again.

parameters.name: NumberType
parameters.description: Which type of number to encode. Can be "Float", "Integer" or "UnsignedInteger".

parameters.name: Bits
parameters.description: The number of bits. Can be 32 or 64 for float. Can be 8, 16, 24, 32 or 64 for integers.

parameters.name: Number
parameters.description: The number to encode.

parameters.name: BigEndian
parameters.description: Pass 1 to encode big endian. Default is 0 for little endian.

examples.label: Encodes a 32-bit floating point number:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.EncodeNumber"; "Float"; 32; 123.456 ) 
// 79E9F642</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>

functions.name: Math.FormatNumber
functions.signature: MBS( "Math.FormatNumber"; Value { ; Digits; Decimal Separator; Thousands Separator } )
functions.description: Formats a number. We format the number and you can specify whether we use comma or dot as decimal separator or detect it automatically from FileMaker's settings. Thousands separator can be given.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The number to format.

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.description: Number of digits. Can be zero when there should be no digits after decimal separator. Default is zero. Maximum 12. Values are rounded.

parameters.name: Decimal Separator
parameters.description: The decimal separator. If empty, we detect the current setting from FileMaker. Can also be explicitly given as "." or ",".

parameters.name: Thousands Separator
parameters.description: The thousands separator. Can be empty. If you pass dot or comma and the same character is used for decimal separator, we switch to the other.

examples.label: Format numbers:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("Math.FormatNumber"; 123) & ¶ &
MBS
("Math.FormatNumber"; 12,34; 2; ",") & ¶ &
MBS
("Math.FormatNumber"; 0,001; 3; "") & ¶ &
MBS
("Math.FormatNumber"; 123456789,1235; 3; ""; ".")
Example result:
123
12,34
0.001
123,456,789.124
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Math.FormatNumber"; 123) & ¶ &
MBS("Math.FormatNumber"; 12,34; 2; ",") & ¶ &
MBS("Math.FormatNumber"; 0,001; 3; "") & ¶ &
MBS("Math.FormatNumber"; 123456789,1235; 3; ""; ".")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Try negative numbers:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("Math.FormatNumber"; -123) & ¶ &
MBS
("Math.FormatNumber"; -12,34; 2; ",") & ¶ &
MBS
("Math.FormatNumber"; -0,001; 3; "") & ¶ &
MBS
("Math.FormatNumber"; -123456789,1235; 3; ""; ".")
Example result:
-123
-12,34
-0.001
-123,456,789.124
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Math.FormatNumber"; -123) & ¶ &
MBS("Math.FormatNumber"; -12,34; 2; ",") & ¶ &
MBS("Math.FormatNumber"; -0,001; 3; "") & ¶ &
MBS("Math.FormatNumber"; -123456789,1235; 3; ""; ".")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Math.GetBit
functions.description: Queries a bit in a 64-bit number. Returns 1 if bit is set or 0 if not.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The current value.

parameters.name: Bit
parameters.description: The bit number to set. From 0 to 63.

examples.label: Query first 8 bits:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 0 ) &
MBS
( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 1 ) &
MBS
( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 2 ) &
MBS
( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 3 ) &
MBS
( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 4 ) &
MBS
( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 5 ) &
MBS
( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 6 ) &
MBS
( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 7 )
Example result:
11011110
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 0 ) & 
MBS( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 1 ) & 
MBS( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 2 ) & 
MBS( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 3 ) &
MBS( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 4 ) &
MBS( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 5 ) &
MBS( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 6 ) &
MBS( "Math.GetBit"; 123; 7 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Calculate Math.GetBit manually using shift and and:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  value = 123;
  bitIndex = 3;
  bit1 = MBS("Math.GetBit"; value; bitIndex );
  mask = MBS("Math.BitwiseShiftLeft"; 1; bitIndex);
  valueMasked = MBS("Math.BitwiseAND"; value; mask);
  bit2 = (valueMasked ≠ 0)
];
bit1 & ¶ & bit2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Math.IsValidCC
functions.description: Verifies a credit card number. This function implements the Luhn algorithm. This is a simple checksum formula used to validate a variety of identification numbers, such as credit card numbers, IMEI numbers, National Provider Identifier numbers in US and Canadian Social Insurance Numbers. If this function returns 0, you can be sure the number is not valid. But if the function returns 1, you may want to check the number online with some database.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text with the number.

functions.result: Returns 1 on success or 0 on failure.

functions.name: Math.Max
functions.description: Calculates maximum of given values. Takes values from arguments, which may be list of values or single values. Returns empty result if no values are passed. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Values parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Values...
parameters.description: Pass an argument for each value or list of values.

Please repeat Values parameter as often as you need.

functions.name: Math.MovingAverage
functions.description: Calculates moving average. We offer simple, weighted and exponential modes. exponential is: Value = (input - Last) * 2/WindowSize + Last exponential2 is: Value = input * 2/(WindowSize+1) + Last * (1-2/(WindowSize+1))

functions.name: Math.Random.ExponentialDistribution
functions.description: Generates a random number using exponential distribution. Produces random non-negative floating-point values, distributed according to probability density function.

parameters.name: Lambda
parameters.description: The lambda value to use.

examples.label: Get a list of 10 values:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While(
	[
		count = 10;
		sum = 0;
		i = 0;
		list = ""
	];
	i < count;
	[
		value = MBS( "Math.Random.ExponentialDistribution"; 1);
		sum = sum + value;
		list = list & value & ¶;
		i = i + 1
	];
	list & ¶ & sum / count
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Math.Rate
functions.signature: MBS( "Math.Rate"; Nper; pmt; pv { ; fv; Type; guess } )
functions.description: Returns the interest rate per period of an annuity. Should be same as Rate() function in Excel. RATE is calculated by iteration and can have zero or more solutions. If the successive results of RATE do not converge to within 0.0000001 after 20 iterations, RATE returns an error. See also: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/rate-function-9f665657-4a7e-4bb7-a030-83fc59e748ce

parameters.name: Nper
parameters.description: The total number of payment periods in an annuity.

parameters.name: pmt
parameters.description: The payment made each period and cannot change over the life of the annuity. Typically, pmt includes principal and interest but no other fees or taxes. If pmt is omitted, you must include the fv argument.

parameters.name: pv
parameters.description: The present value — the total amount that a series of future payments is worth now.

parameters.name: fv
parameters.description: The future value, or a cash balance you want to attain after the last payment is made. If fv is omitted, it is assumed to be 0 (the future value of a loan, for example, is 0). If fv is omitted, you must include the pmt argument.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The number 0 or 1 and indicates when payments are due. 0 or omitted: At the end of the period 1: At the beginning of the period

parameters.name: guess
parameters.description: Your guess for what the rate will be. * If you omit guess, it is assumed to be 10 percent. * If RATE does not converge, try different values for guess. RATE usually converges if guess is between 0 and 1.

examples.label: Do a test calculation:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$LoanAmount
; Value: 8000 ]
Set Variable [
$MonthlyPaymentAmount
; Value: 152,5 ]
Set Variable [
$NumberOfYears
; Value: 5 ]
Set Variable [
$NumberOfPeriods
; Value: 60 ]
Set Variable [
$PaymentPeriodsPerYear
; Value: 12 ]
Set Variable [
$Rate
; Value:
MBS
("Math.Rate";
$NumberOfPeriods
; -
$MonthlyPaymentAmount
;
$LoanAmount
) ]
Set Variable [
$Rate
; Value: Round (
$Rate
* 100; 2 ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Rate" ; "Monthly: " &
$Rate
& "%" // shows .45% & ¶ & "Annually: " & (
$Rate
*
$PaymentPeriodsPerYear
) & "%" ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>8000 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$LoanAmount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>152,5 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$MonthlyPaymentAmount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>5 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NumberOfYears</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>60 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NumberOfPeriods</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>12 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PaymentPeriodsPerYear</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Math.Rate"; $NumberOfPeriods; -$MonthlyPaymentAmount; $LoanAmount) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Rate</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Round ( $Rate * 100; 2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Rate</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Rate" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Monthly: " & $Rate & "%" // shows .45% & ¶ & "Annually: " & ($Rate * $PaymentPeriodsPerYear) & "%" </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Calculate yearly rate:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
LoanAmount = 14076;
MonthlyPaymentAmount = 264.34;
NumberOfYears = 5;
NumberOfPeriods = 60;
PaymentPeriodsPerYear = 12;
Rate = MBS("Math.Rate"; NumberOfPeriods; -MonthlyPaymentAmount; LoanAmount); // monthly
Rate = ((1 + Rate)^12-1); // yearly
Rate = Round ( Rate * 100; 2 ) // rounded
]; Rate)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Calculate rate with end value:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
LoanAmount = -13324.46;
MonthlyPaymentAmount = 181.34;
NumberOfYears = 5;
NumberOfPeriods = 60;
PaymentPeriodsPerYear = 12;
EndValue = 5196.19;
Rate = MBS("Math.Rate"; NumberOfPeriods; MonthlyPaymentAmount; LoanAmount; EndValue; 0); // monthly
RateYearly = ((1 + Rate)^12-1); // yearly
RateYearly = Round ( RateYearly * 100; 2 ) // rounded
]; RateYearly)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MountNotification.Install
functions.description: Installs a script trigger to be called when a disk mounts/unmounts. Parameter to the script will contain details in a list with several values. First value is typ of call, "mount" or "unmount". Second value is native path, on Mac e.g. "/Volumes/TestDisk" and on Windows a drive letter like "E:\". On Mac third value is user visible name and forth parameter is URL for mount point. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

examples.label: Installs notification script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MountNotification.Install"; "test.fp7"; "MyScript" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

If you disable dialog for the FileMake Script step, the plugin has no way to intercept the printing. Use Printer.SetPrinter for similar features on Windows.

examples.label: Run dialog with plugin:
examples.text_code:
Allow User Abort [ Off ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PageSetupDialog.Install
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("PageSetupDialog.Enable") ]
# set options here
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay
"; ,1; "
MBS
( \"
Menubar.RunMenuCommand
\"; 57606 )") // run Page Setup Dialog in
0.1
seconds ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="85" name="Allow User Abort">
    <Set state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PageSetupDialog.Install") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PageSetupDialog.Enable") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set options here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; ,1; "MBS( \"Menubar.RunMenuCommand\"; 57606 )") // run Page Setup Dialog in 0.1 seconds </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.description: Queries paper name. You can set this with PageSetupDialog.SetPaperName .

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.GetPaperOrientation
functions.description: Queries paper orientation. Result should be landscape, portrait or default.

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.GetPaperWidth
functions.description: Queries paper width to set. Value is -1 if not set. Use PageSetupDialog.SetPaperWidth to set this.

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.GetPrinterName
functions.description: Queries printer name. You can set this with PageSetupDialog.SetPrinterName .

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.GetScalingFactor
functions.description: Queries scaling factor.

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.Install
functions.description: Installs the extension for the page setup dialog. Only after PageSetupDialog.Install was called, you can use PageSetupDialog.Enable . You only call this function once. This is Mac only. For Windows, please check Printer.SetPrinter and Printer.Print .

examples.label: Initialize paper setup dialog:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PageSetupDialog.Install") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Run dialog with plugin:
examples.text_code:
Allow User Abort [ Off ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("PageSetupDialog.Install") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PageSetupDialog.Enable
") ]
# set options here
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay
"; ,1; "
MBS
( \"
Menubar.RunMenuCommand
\"; 57606 )") // run Page Setup Dialog in
0.1
seconds ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="85" name="Allow User Abort">
    <Set state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PageSetupDialog.Install") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PageSetupDialog.Enable") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set options here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; ,1; "MBS( \"Menubar.RunMenuCommand\"; 57606 )") // run Page Setup Dialog in 0.1 seconds </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.Reset
functions.description: Resets settings in plugin. We reset our properties back to defaults. Orientation, ScalingFactor, PaperWidth and PaperHeight are all set to -1. NoDialog is set to off. PrinterName, PaperName, Options and LastSettings are cleared.

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.SetNoDialog
functions.description: Sets whether dialog should show. In FileMaker script step you need to use page setup with dialog. Then the plugin can control the dialog and either close it right away (with setting 1) or let it open (setting 0).

parameters.name: Flag
parameters.description: Whether to show page setup dialog.

examples.label: Set to show dialog:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PageSetupDialog.SetNoDialog"; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: No dialog, please:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PageSetupDialog.SetNoDialog"; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.SetOption
functions.description: Sets an option. You can use PrintDialog.GetLastSettings after printing to query XML with all settings and pick keys and values.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key to use.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to use. Depending on data type, the plugin will pass a number or a text.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of data to add. Default is empty to use type of value passed. So text or number type would be picked automatically. Valid values for type are text, number, integer and text. Integer is for integer numbers while number is for floating numbers.

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.SetPaperHeight
functions.description: Sets paper height to use. If paper width and height are both set, we pass this to the dialog for a custom paper size. Value is -1 if not set.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value. Unit is in 72 dpi. e.g. 10 cm is 10 / 2.54 * 72 = 283 point.

We found paper sizes for our printer in PPD files in /private/etc/cups/ppd folder. e.g. "Env10" for Envelope #10.

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.SetPrinterName
functions.description: Sets printer name. The plugin will try to find a printer with the given name and assigns it for the printing. You need to run paper setup dialog to let the plugin change values. The printer set for paper setup may not be the one used for printing. The dialog will not show the one you select here, if you show dialog.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The printer name to use.

functions.name: PageSetupDialog.SetScalingFactor
functions.description: Sets scaling factor. If zero, we don't change it. Value is 1.0 for 100%. You need to run paper setup dialog to let the plugin change values.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new scale factor.

examples.label: Set scaling to 150%:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PageSetupDialog.SetScalingFactor"; 1,5) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SmartCard.Available
functions.description: Whether SmartCard API is available. Should always be available on Mac and Windows. Returns 1 if available or 0 if not. On a server it would read the smart card attached the to the server, not any client.

functions.name: SmartCard.BeginTransaction
functions.description: Starts a transaction. The function waits for the completion of all other transactions before it begins. After the transaction starts, all other applications are blocked from accessing the smart card while the transaction is in progress. If a transaction is held on the card for more than five seconds with no operations happening on that card, then the card is reset. Calling any of the Smart Card and Reader Access Functions or Direct Card Access Functions on the card that is transacted results in the timer being reset to continue allowing the transaction to be used. The BeginTransaction function is a smart card and reader access function. For more information about other access functions, see Smart Card and Reader Access Functions.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

functions.name: SmartCard.Cancel
functions.description: Terminates all outstanding actions within a specific resource manager context. The only requests that you can cancel are those that require waiting for external action by the smart card or user. Any such outstanding action requests will terminate with a status indication that the action was canceled. This is especially useful to force outstanding GetStatusChange calls to terminate.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

functions.name: SmartCard.CancelTransaction
functions.description: Cancels current transaction.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

functions.name: SmartCard.Connect
functions.description: Connects to a smartcard. Establishes a connection (using a specific resource manager context) between the calling application and a smart card contained by a specific reader. If no card exists in the specified reader, an error is returned. On success we have a connection to a smart card. You can query SmartCard.GetActiveProtocol

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: Reader
parameters.description: The name of the reader that contains the target card.

parameters.name: ShareMode
parameters.description: A flag that indicates whether other applications may form connections to the card. Can be integer or one of the following texts: Exclusive (1), Shared (2) or Direct (3).

parameters.name: PreferredProtocols
parameters.description: A number with bitmask of acceptable protocols for the connection or just the text name of the value for only one protocol. Pass T0 (1), T1 (2), T15 (8, not on Windows), Any (3), RAW (4).

examples.label: Connect
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SmartCard.Connect"; $Context; $Reader; "Shared"; "any" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SmartCard.Control
functions.description: Direct control of the reader. You can call it any time after a successful call to SmartCard.Connect and before a successful call to SmartCard.Disconnect . The effect on the state of the reader depends on the control code.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: ControlCode
parameters.description: Control code for the operation. This value identifies the specific operation to be performed.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: Hex encoded input data. Contains the data required to perform the operation.

parameters.name: OutputSize
parameters.description: The expected output size. This is the buffer size the plugin will use for receiving.

functions.result: Returns hex encoded data or error.

functions.name: SmartCard.Disconnect
functions.description: Disconnects card. Terminates a connection previously opened between the calling application and a smart card in the target reader.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: Disposition
parameters.description: Action to take on the card in the connected reader on close. Can be Leave, Reset, Unpower or Eject.

examples.label: Close it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SmartCard.Disconnect"; $card; "leave")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SmartCard.EndTransaction
functions.description: Ends transaction. Completes a previously declared transaction, allowing other applications to resume interactions with the card.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: Disposition
parameters.description: Action to take on the card in the connected reader on close. Can be Eject, Leave, Reset or Unpower.

functions.name: SmartCard.GetActiveProtocol
functions.description: Queries the active protocol. The result is undefined, Raw, T0, T1 or T15.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

functions.name: SmartCard.GetAttribute
functions.description: Retrieves the current reader attribute. It does not affect the state of the reader, driver, or card. The plugin first queries for size of attribute and on success queries again to get the data and return it as a hex encoded text.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: AttributeID
parameters.description: The ID of the attribute.

functions.name: SmartCard.HeaderTemplate
functions.description: Queries header template.

parameters.name: Protocol
parameters.description: Which protocol you need a header. Can be T0, T1 or RAW.

examples.label: Get T0 header:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SmartCard.HeaderTemplate"; "T0" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Get T1 header:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SmartCard.HeaderTemplate"; "T1" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SmartCard.Init
functions.description: Initializes a smartcard context. Returns the ID for other SmartCard functions. Please call SmartCard.Release later.
functions.result: Returns SmartCard Context ID or error.

examples.label: List readers:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
( "SmartCard.Init" )]
Set Variable [
$readers
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.ListReaders
";
$c
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Release
";

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS( "SmartCard.Available" ) = 1]
	#Start a new session
	Set Variable [$SCContext; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Init" )]
	If [MBS( "SmartCard.Valid"; $SCContext )]
		#Query list of readers and pick first
		Set Variable [$SCReader; Value:GetValue ( MBS( "SmartCard.ListReaders"; $SCContext) ; 1 )]
		If [MBS("IsError") = 0]
			# Connect to card
			Set Variable [$SCConnect; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Connect"; $SCContext ; $SCReader ; "Shared"; "any")]
			Set Variable [$PRotocol; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.GetActiveProtocol"; $SCContext)]
			If [$SCConnect = "OK"]
				#Run a query
				Set Variable [$Result; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Transmit"; $SCContext; "0200000008000000"; "00ca018202"; 512 )]
				#Result is 4 bytes. Starts with C901 for Version 5.0 or C903 for Version 5.3
				Show Custom Dialog ["Version"; $result]
				#Disconnect
				Set Variable [$SCDisconnect; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Disconnect"; $SCContext; "Leave" )]
			Else
				Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "No card available."]
			End If
		End If
		#End Card Session
		Set Variable [$SCRelease; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Release" ; $SCContext )]
	Else
		Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "Failed to initalize"]
	End If
Else
	Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "No SmartCard API on this OS."]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query name from Swiss health card:
examples.text_code:
# Initialize smart card:
Set Variable [
$$SmartCard
; Value:
MBS
( "SmartCard.Init" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to initialize SmartCard" ;
$$SmartCard
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
#
# Connect to a reader
Set Variable [
$reader
; Value: "Identiv SCR3310 uTrust 2700 R" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Connect
";
$$SmartCard
;
$Reader
; "Shared"; "any" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Connect failed" ;
$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
#
# Read file from smartcard with APDU protocol.
#
# Read record with name
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.ReadFile
";
$$SmartCard
; "2F06") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Field [
Card::Status
;
$result
]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error" ;
$result
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# Pick result
Set Variable [
$filedata
; Value: Middle (
$result
; 5; Length(
$result
) ) ]
#
# Split in values
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
("
SmartCard.SplitValues
";
$FileData
) ]
# Get item 128, which is full name
Set Variable [
$name
; Value: JSONGetElement (
$json
; "128" ) ]
Set Variable [
$NameList
; Value: Substitute(
$name
; ", "; ¶) ]
Set Variable [
$LastName
; Value: GetValue(
$nameList
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$FirstName
; Value: GetValue(
$nameList
; 2) ]
#
Show Custom Dialog [ "Name" ;
$FirstName
& " " &
$LastName
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Disconnect
";
$$SmartCard
; "Eject") ]

functions.name: SmartCard.ListReaderGroups
functions.description: Queries list of reader groups. Provides the list of reader groups that have previously been introduced to the system. The groups may be SCard$AllReaders, SCard$DefaultReaders, SCard$LocalReaders and SCard$SystemReaders.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

functions.name: SmartCard.PerformSignature
functions.description: Performs signature. Convenience function which wraps a few calls to Transmit function. With plugin version 7.1 the length of hash is variable. This is function for atos Smartcards CardOS v5.0/5.3, which we needed for point of sales software in Austria. It may not work with your cards unless they have same protocol.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: DeviceType
parameters.description: Which device to handle, so the plugin talks the right dialect. Currently supported cardos and acos.

parameters.name: Hash
parameters.description: The input hash to sign. Length should be 40 hex characters, so it's decoded 20 bytes.

parameters.name: ReturnType
parameters.description: What format to return the certificate. Can be Hex or Base64. Default is hex.

functions.name: SmartCard.ReadCertificate
functions.description: Reads certificate for smart cards. Convenience function which wraps a few calls to Transmit function. This is function for atos Smartcards CardOS v5.0/5.3, which we needed for point of sales software in Austria. It may not work with your cards unless they have same protocol.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: DeviceType
parameters.description: Which device to handle, so the plugin talks the right dialect. Currently supported cardos and acos.

parameters.name: ReturnType
parameters.description: What format to return the certificate. Can be Hex or Base64. Default is hex.

functions.name: SmartCard.ReadFile
functions.description: Read file from smart card with APDU protocol. Returns data in desired encoding or error message. The first two bytes (4 Hex characters) are status code. For JPEG we remove status code to return only image. For Swiss health cards, the relevant IDs are 2F06 and 2F07. For Belgian EID:

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: FileID
parameters.description: The fileID of the file to retrieve. Given as hex string.

parameters.name: ResultType
parameters.description: How to return data. Can be JPEG, Hex or Base64.

functions.name: SmartCard.Reconnect
functions.description: Reconnects to smart card with different protocol. The Reconnect function reestablishes an existing connection between the calling application and a smart card. This function moves a card handle from direct access to general access, or acknowledges and clears an error condition that is preventing further access to the card. On success we have a connection to a smart card. You can query SmartCard.GetActiveProtocol

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: ShareMode
parameters.description: A flag that indicates whether other applications may form connections to the card. Can be integer or one of the following texts: Exclusive (1), Shared (2) or Direct (3).

parameters.name: PreferredProtocols
parameters.description: A number with bitmask of acceptable protocols for the connection or just the text name of the value for only one protocol. Pass T0 (1), T1 (2), T15 (8, not on Windows), Any (3), RAW (4).

parameters.name: Initialization
parameters.description: Type of initialization that should be performed on the card. Can be Leave, Reset, Unpower or Eject.

functions.name: SmartCard.Release
functions.description: Releases context and all memory needed. Also frees any smart card object.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

functions.name: SmartCard.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Frees all SmartCard objects. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SmartCard.SetAttribute
functions.description: Sets the given reader attribute. It does not affect the state of the reader, reader driver, or smart card. Not all attributes are supported by all readers (nor can they be set at all times) as many of the attributes are under direct control of the transport protocol.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: AttributeID
parameters.description: The ID of the attribute.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The hex encoded data to be set.

functions.name: SmartCard.SplitValues
functions.description: Split values read from smart card. Files on smartcard can contain key/value data. Returns JSON with an object containing numbers as text (key) and values as text. Sample for JSON result: {

parameters.name: Input
parameters.description: The hex encoded input string.

functions.name: SmartCard.Status
functions.description: Queries current status of smart card in reader. You can call it any time after a successful call to SmartCard.Connect and before a successful call to SmartCard.Disconnect . It does not affect the state of the reader or reader driver. If you ask for State, the result is Absent, Present, Swallowed, Powered, Negotiable or Specific.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to query. Can be ReaderName, State, Protocol or ATR.

examples.label: Query reader name:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SmartCard.Status"; $SmartCardContext; "ReaderName" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SmartCard.Transmit
functions.description: Sends a service request to the smart card and expects to receive data back from the card. Returns receive buffer hex encoded. see also https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa379804(v=vs.85).aspx You can use SmartCard.TransmitReceiveHeader to return last Transmit's receive header.

parameters.name: SmartCardContext
parameters.description: The smartcard context reference number.

parameters.name: SendHeader
parameters.description: The hex encoded protocol header structure for the instruction. This buffer is in the format of an SCARD_IO_REQUEST structure, followed by the specific protocol control information (PCI). For the T=0, T=1, and Raw protocols, the PCI structure is constant. The smart card subsystem supplies a global T=0, T=1, or Raw PCI structure, which you can reference by using the symbols T0, T1, and RAW respectively. With version 7.0 or newert this can be empty to use default header.

parameters.name: SendBuffer
parameters.description: The hex encoded data to be sent to the card. For T=0, the data parameters are placed into the address pointed to by pbSendBuffer according to the following structure: Type	name	Description Byte	Cla	The instruction class Byte	Ins	The instruction code Byte	P1	Parameter to the instruction Byte	P2	Parameter to the instruction Byte	P3	Size of I/O transfer The data sent to the card should immediately follow the send buffer. In the special case where no data is sent to the card and no data is expected in return, P3 is not sent.

parameters.name: RecvLength
parameters.description: The number of bytes for the receiving buffer. How many bytes the plugin allocates for the answer. Can be much bigger than what you expect.

examples.label: Connect and query value:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Available
" ) = 1]
#Start a new session
Set Variable [
$SCContext
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Init
" )]
If [
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Valid
";
$SCContext
)]
#Query list of readers and pick first
Set Variable [
$SCReader
; Value:GetValue (
MBS
( "
SmartCard.ListReaders
";
$SCContext
) ; 1 )]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0]
# Connect to card
Set Variable [
$SCConnect
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Connect
";
$SCContext
;
$SCReader
; "Shared"; "any")]
Set Variable [
$PRotocol
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.GetActiveProtocol
";
$SCContext
)]
If [
$SCConnect
= "OK"]
#Run a query
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
( "SmartCard.Transmit";
$SCContext
; "0200000008000000"; "00ca018202"; 512 )]
#Result is 4 bytes. Starts with C901 for Version
5.0
or C903 for Version
5.3
Show Custom Dialog ["Version";
$result
]
#Disconnect
Set Variable [
$SCDisconnect
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Disconnect
";
$SCContext
; "Leave" )]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "No card available."]
End If
End If
#End Card Session
Set Variable [
$SCRelease
; Value:
MBS
( "
SmartCard.Release
" ;
$SCContext
)]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "Failed to initalize"]
End If
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "No SmartCard API on this OS."]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS( "SmartCard.Available" ) = 1]
	#Start a new session
	Set Variable [$SCContext; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Init" )]
	If [MBS( "SmartCard.Valid"; $SCContext )]
		#Query list of readers and pick first
		Set Variable [$SCReader; Value:GetValue ( MBS( "SmartCard.ListReaders"; $SCContext) ; 1 )]
		If [MBS("IsError") = 0]
			# Connect to card
			Set Variable [$SCConnect; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Connect"; $SCContext ; $SCReader ; "Shared"; "any")]
			Set Variable [$PRotocol; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.GetActiveProtocol"; $SCContext)]
			If [$SCConnect = "OK"]
				#Run a query
				Set Variable [$Result; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Transmit"; $SCContext; "0200000008000000"; "00ca018202"; 512 )]
				#Result is 4 bytes. Starts with C901 for Version 5.0 or C903 for Version 5.3
				Show Custom Dialog ["Version"; $result]
				#Disconnect
				Set Variable [$SCDisconnect; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Disconnect"; $SCContext; "Leave" )]
			Else
				Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "No card available."]
			End If
		End If
		#End Card Session
		Set Variable [$SCRelease; Value:MBS( "SmartCard.Release" ; $SCContext )]
	Else
		Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "Failed to initalize"]
	End If
Else
	Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "No SmartCard API on this OS."]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

MBS

Plugin.LicenseeName

; Value:

functions.name: Updater.CheckForUpdates
functions.description: Explicitly checks for updates and displays a progress dialog while doing so.

functions.name: Updater.CheckForUpdatesInBackground
functions.description: Checks for updates, but does not display any UI unless an update is found. This is meant for programmatically initating a check for updates. That is, it will display no UI unless it actually finds an update, in which case it proceeds as usual. This function checks for a license. Created 29th September 2016 , last changed 29th September 2016 Updater.CheckForUpdates - Updater.GetAutomaticallyChecksForUpdates 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Updater.GetAutomaticallyChecksForUpdates
functions.description: Queries whether automatic update check is enabled. You may use this to detect if FileMaker told the updater to check automatically for updates.

functions.name: Updater.GetAutomaticallyDownloadsUpdates
functions.description: Queries whether automatic update download is enabled. This is by default off in FileMaker Pro.

functions.name: Updater.GetFeedURL
functions.description: Queries the URL of the update feed. This is an URL for a XML file which provides update details. If you get this URL and download the XML file, you can find the download URLs for the updaters. This function checks for a license. Created 29th September 2016 , last changed 29th September 2016 Updater.GetAutomaticallyDownloadsUpdates - Updater.GetSendsSystemProfile 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Updater.GetSendsSystemProfile
functions.description: Queries whether to send system profile while updating. This is off by default in FileMaker 15.

functions.name: Updater.LastUpdateCheckDate
functions.description: Queries the last update check timestamp. Your solution can query this date and check if it should complain to user about not checking for updates for a long time. Returns empty result if not known. This function checks for a license. Created 29th September 2016 , last changed 29th September 2016 Updater.GetSendsSystemProfile - Updater.SetAutomaticallyChecksForUpdates 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Updater.SetAutomaticallyChecksForUpdates
functions.description: Turns automatically checking for updates on or off. Changing this only influences the Sparkle framework doing the updates, not the FileMaker preferences.

parameters.name: Flag
parameters.description: The new flag. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: Updater.SetAutomaticallyDownloadsUpdates
functions.description: Sets whether automatic update download is enabled. Changing this only influences the Sparkle framework doing the updates, not the FileMaker preferences.

parameters.name: Flag
parameters.description: The new flag. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: Updater.SetSendsSystemProfile
functions.description: Sets whether to send system profile while updating. Changing this only influences the Sparkle framework doing the updates, not the FileMaker preferences.

parameters.name: Flag
parameters.description: The new flag. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: Updater.UpdateInProgress
functions.description: Queries whether an update is in progress. This function checks for a license. Created 29th September 2016 , last changed 29th September 2016 Updater.SetSendsSystemProfile - UserNotification.ClearDidActivateNotificationScript 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.Cancel
functions.description: Cancels download. Make sure you cleanup your stuff like a progress dialog.
functions.result: Returns OK on success or error.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.ClearBeginDownloadHandler
functions.description: Clears the script called for beginning downloads.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.ClearDidDownloadHandler
functions.description: Clears the script called for finished downloads.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.ClearFailHandler
functions.description: Clears the script called for failed downloads.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.ClearFolder
functions.description: Clears the download folder. Next download will go to temporary folder (which is default).

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.ClearProgressHandler
functions.description: Clears the script called for download progress.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.DownloadedLength
functions.description: Number of bytes downloaded so far. Useful to show progress together with WebDownloadDelegate.expectedContentLength .

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.ErrorMessage
functions.description: The last error message from download. Typically you read this in your registered download fail script.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.GetFolder
functions.description: Queries current defined download folder. Returns empty text if default folder is used from system (temp folder).

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.Install
functions.description: Assigns this web viewer our download delegate. This needs to made whenever you want to receive download script calls for a web viewer. If web viewer is part of a data record in FileMaker, you may need to set it for each record whenever a record is loaded. Implemented for WebKit 2.x in v13.4.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function. Only needed for WebKit 1!

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error.

examples.label: Makes sure all PDF files are downloaded:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebDownloadDelegate.Install" ; "web" )
MBS( "WebPolicyDelegate.AddMimeType"; "application/pdf"; "download" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Install for WebKit 2:
examples.text_code:
# must run before web viewer is created
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebDownloadDelegate.Install") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> must run before web viewer is created</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebDownloadDelegate.Install") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.MIMEType
functions.description: Returns the MIME type. The MIME type is often provided by the response's originating source. However, that value may be changed or corrected by a protocol implementation if it can be determined that the response's source reported the information incorrectly. If the response's originating source does not provide a MIME type, an attempt to guess the MIME type may be made.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.Path
functions.description: The file path of the downloaded file. This is a temporary file and you should import, move, rename or copy it. If you don't delete this file, it will be on the next restart of the Mac. You may need to use Files.MoveFile later to move the file to the place you like. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.SetBeginDownloadHandler
functions.description: Sets the script called when a download is about to begin. You can show progress dialog if you like. Properties are not yet set, so you can only show that download started and wait for other script calls. The plugin only supports one download at a time. Still you could have several, but functions like WebDownloadDelegate.DownloadedLength will not work correct. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers fail handler:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebDownloadDelegate.SetBeginDownloadHandler"; Get ( FileName ); "DownloadBegin")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.SetDidDownloadHandler
functions.description: Sets the script called when a download finished. Use WebDownloadDelegate.URL to learn which URL has been loaded. Use WebDownloadDelegate.Path to learn where file has been created. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers fail handler:
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebDownloadDelegate.SetDidDownloadHandler"; Get ( FileName ); "DownloadFinished")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.SetFailHandler
functions.description: Sets the script called when a download failed. Use WebDownloadDelegate.ErrorMessage to get a localized error message. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers fail handler:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebDownloadDelegate.SetFailHandler"; Get ( FileName ); "DownloadFailed")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.SetFolder
functions.description: Sets the download folder. Make sure the folder is empty as with duplicate file name you get an error. If you do not set download folder, it goes to default temporary folder. Version 9.5 or newer check if folder is valid before setting it. Sadly macOS Catalina may ignore this folder setting and still use temporary folder, so you may need to use Files.MoveFile later to move the file to the place you like. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the download folder.

examples.label: Download to desktop:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("WebDownloadDelegate.SetFolder";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" )) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebDownloadDelegate.SetFolder"; MBS( "Folders.UserDesktop" )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.SetProgressHandler
functions.description: Sets the script called when a download made progress. This event is called at maximum once a second. Use WebDownloadDelegate.URL to learn which URL is loading. Use WebDownloadDelegate.Path to learn where file has been created. Use WebDownloadDelegate.expectedContentLength to learn how much data is expected and WebDownloadDelegate.DownloadedLength how much data has already been loaded. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers fail handler:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebDownloadDelegate.SetProgressHandler"; Get ( FileName ); "DownloadFailed")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.URL
functions.description: Queries current URL. This is first the requested URL, but can later be the URL where we got redirected to.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.expectedContentLength
functions.description: Returns the expected content length. Some protocol implementations report the content length as part of the response, but not all protocols guarantee to deliver that amount of data. Clients should be prepared to deal with more or less data.
functions.result: Returns the expected content length, or -1 if the length can't be determined.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.suggestedFilename
functions.description: Returns a suggested filename. The method tries to create a filename using the following, in order: A filename specified using the content disposition header. The last path component of the URL. The host of the URL. If the host of URL can't be converted to a valid filename, the filename "unknown" is used. In most cases, this method appends the proper file extension based on the MIME type. This method will always return a valid filename regardless of whether or not the resource is saved to disk.

functions.name: WebDownloadDelegate.textEncodingName
functions.description: Returns the name of the receiver's text encoding provided by the response's originating source. This function is free to use. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 26th August 2023 WebDownloadDelegate.suggestedFilename - WebHook.Check 💬 Ask a question or report a problem
functions.result: The name of the receiver's text encoding provided by the response's originating source, or empty text if no text encoding was provided by the protocol.
